---
url: https://opendal.apache.org/docs/bindings/java/
---
# Java | Apache OpenDAL™

# Apache OpenDAL™ Java Binding

A native Java binding for OpenDAL: access S3, GCS, Azure Blob, HDFS, the local filesystem, and [50+ more services](/services/) through one API, with the performance of the Rust core underneath.

New to the model behind the API? Read [Concepts](/docs/concepts/) first — service, operator, layer, operation are the same four ideas in every language.

note

This binding has its own version number, independent of the Rust core. Check compatibility against the binding's version, not the core's.

## Status[​](#status "Direct link to Status")

Released and stable, published to[Maven Central](https://central.sonatype.com/search?q=opendal&smo=true) under the `org.apache.opendal` group.

## Capabilities[​](#capabilities "Direct link to Capabilities")

* **Sync and async** — use [Operator](https://opendal.apache.org/docs/java/org/apache/opendal/Operator.html) for blocking calls or [AsyncOperator](https://opendal.apache.org/docs/java/org/apache/opendal/AsyncOperator.html)for `CompletableFuture`\-based calls, with the same verbs.
* **Typed or map-based config** — build from a typed `ServiceConfig` builder or a plain `Map<String, String>`.
* **All services included** — the native library bundles every backend; there are no build flags to enable.
* **Presigned URLs** for services that support them (async API).

## Installation[​](#installation "Direct link to Installation")

The binding ships a platform-specific native library, so you depend on the main artifact plus a classifier for your platform. The simplest setup uses an OS detector plugin to fill in the classifier automatically.

### Maven[​](#maven "Direct link to Maven")

```

<build>

<extensions>

  <extension>

    <groupId>kr.motd.maven</groupId>

    <artifactId>os-maven-plugin</artifactId>

    <version>1.7.0</version>

  </extension>

</extensions>

</build>


<dependencies>

  <dependency>

    <groupId>org.apache.opendal</groupId>

    <artifactId>opendal</artifactId>

    <version>${opendal.version}</version>

  </dependency>

  <dependency>

    <groupId>org.apache.opendal</groupId>

    <artifactId>opendal</artifactId>

    <version>${opendal.version}</version>

    <classifier>${os.detected.classifier}</classifier>

  </dependency>

</dependencies>


```

### Gradle[​](#gradle "Direct link to Gradle")

```

plugins {

    id "com.google.osdetector" version "1.7.3"

}


dependencies {

    implementation "org.apache.opendal:opendal:$opendalVersion"

    implementation "org.apache.opendal:opendal:$opendalVersion:$osdetector.classifier"

}


```

On musl-based Linux distributions such as Alpine, use the `linux-x86_64-musl` or`linux-aarch_64-musl` classifier instead of the detected one.

## Useful Links[​](#useful-links "Direct link to Useful Links")

* **API reference**: [opendal.apache.org/docs/java](https://opendal.apache.org/docs/java/)
* **Services & configuration**: [/services](/services/)
* **Upgrade guide**: [upgrade.md](https://github.com/apache/opendal/blob/main/bindings/java/upgrade.md)
* **Source & examples**: [bindings/java/](https://github.com/apache/opendal/tree/main/bindings/java)

## Next steps[​](#next-steps "Direct link to Next steps")

1. [Getting started](/docs/bindings/java/getting-started/) — a runnable program, then a real backend.
2. [Connecting to your storage](/docs/bindings/java/connecting/) — build an operator for any service.
3. [Common tasks](/docs/bindings/java/tasks/) — read, write, stream, list, presign, and more.
4. [Going to production](/docs/bindings/java/production/) — retries, errors, and capability checks.

# Lua | Apache OpenDAL™

# Apache OpenDAL™ Lua Binding

A Lua binding for OpenDAL: access the local filesystem, in-memory storage, S3, and [50+ more services](/services/) through one API, backed by the Rust core.

New to the model behind the API? Read [Concepts](/docs/concepts/) first — service, operator, layer, operation are the same four ideas in every language.

Experimental / WIP

This binding is **unreleased and experimental**. APIs may change without notice. It is not yet published to LuaRocks. Use it for evaluation and contribution, not for production workloads.

## Status[​](#status "Direct link to Status")

Experimental / work in progress. Not yet published to LuaRocks. The binding version is independent of the Rust core version.

## Capabilities[​](#capabilities "Direct link to Capabilities")

* **Blocking API** — all operations are synchronous from Lua's perspective.
* **String-based I/O** — `write` takes a Lua string; `read` returns a Lua string.
* **Core operations** — `read`, `write`, `delete`, `stat`, `rename`,`create_dir`, `is_exist`.
* **Metadata** — `content_length`, `is_file`, `is_dir` from `stat` results.
* **Any service** that OpenDAL's Rust core supports, including `fs`, `memory`, S3, GCS, and the full list at [/services](/services/).

Not yet available: async API, listing, streaming, presigned URLs, layers.

## Build and install[​](#build-and-install "Direct link to Build and install")

LuaRocks publishing is planned but not yet done. Build from source:

```

# Step 1: build the native library (default Lua version is 5.2).

cd bindings/lua

cargo build --package opendal-lua --release


# Step 2: copy it to your Lua shared-library directory.

# Adjust the path for your Lua version and OS.

cp ../../target/release/libopendal_lua.so /usr/lib/lua/5.2/opendal.so


```

Alternatively, use LuaRocks with the bundled rockspec:

```

cd bindings/lua

luarocks make


```

**Lua version**: the binding currently supports Lua 5.2 only. The default feature is `lua52`. Other Lua versions (`lua51`, `lua53`, `lua54`,`luajit`, `luau`) are not yet wired up as Cargo features in this binding.

## Useful Links[​](#useful-links "Direct link to Useful Links")

* **User guide**: [opendal.apache.org/docs/bindings/lua](https://opendal.apache.org/docs/bindings/lua)
* **Services & configuration**: [/services](/services/)
* **Source & examples**: [bindings/lua/](https://github.com/apache/opendal/tree/main/bindings/lua)

## Next steps[​](#next-steps "Direct link to Next steps")

1. [Getting started](/docs/bindings/lua/getting-started/) — create an operator, write, read, and inspect metadata.

---
url: https://opendal.apache.org/docs/bindings/nodejs/

# Node.js | Apache OpenDAL™

# Apache OpenDAL™ Node.js Binding

A native Node.js binding for OpenDAL: access S3, GCS, Azure Blob, HDFS, the local filesystem, and [50+ more services](/services/) through one API, with the performance of the Rust core underneath.

New to the model behind the API? Read [Concepts](/docs/concepts/) first — service, operator, layer, operation are the same four ideas in every language.

note

This binding has its own version number, independent of the Rust core. Check compatibility against the binding's version, not the core's.

## Status[​](#status "Direct link to Status")

Released and stable, published to [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/opendal).

## Capabilities[​](#capabilities "Direct link to Capabilities")

* **Promise-based async API** — every operation returns a `Promise`; `await` it.
* **Synchronous variants** — each verb has a `*Sync` form (`readSync`,`writeSync`, …) for blocking code.
* **Streaming** reads and writes via Node `Readable`/`Writable` streams for data that does not fit in memory.
* **All services included** — the package bundles every backend; there are no build flags to enable.
* **Composable layers** for retry, timeout, logging, throttle, and concurrency limits — see [Going to production](/docs/bindings/nodejs/production/).
* **Presigned URLs** for services that support them (S3, GCS, Azblob, …).

## Installation[​](#installation "Direct link to Installation")

```

npm install opendal


```

## Useful Links[​](#useful-links "Direct link to Useful Links")

* **API reference**: [opendal.apache.org/docs/nodejs](https://opendal.apache.org/docs/nodejs/)
* **Services & configuration**: [/services](/services/)
* **Upgrade guide**: [upgrade.md](https://github.com/apache/opendal/blob/main/bindings/nodejs/upgrade.md)
* **Source & examples**: [bindings/nodejs/](https://github.com/apache/opendal/tree/main/bindings/nodejs) · [bindings/nodejs/examples/](https://github.com/apache/opendal/tree/main/bindings/nodejs/examples)

## Next steps[​](#next-steps "Direct link to Next steps")

1. [Getting started](/docs/bindings/nodejs/getting-started/) — a runnable program, then a real backend.
2. [Connecting to your storage](/docs/bindings/nodejs/connecting/) — build an operator for any service.
3. [Common tasks](/docs/bindings/nodejs/tasks/) — read, write, stream, list, copy, and presign.
4. [Going to production](/docs/bindings/nodejs/production/) — retry, timeouts, errors, observability.

# OCaml | Apache OpenDAL™

# Apache OpenDAL™ OCaml Binding

An OCaml binding for OpenDAL: access S3, GCS, Azure Blob, the local filesystem, and [50+ more services](/services/) through one API, backed by the Rust core.

New to the model behind the API? Read [Concepts](/docs/concepts/) first — service, operator, layer, and operation are the same four ideas in every language.

Experimental / Work in Progress

This binding is **unreleased and experimental**. APIs may change without notice. It is not published to opam. Build from source only.

## Status[​](#status "Direct link to Status")

Unreleased — work in progress. OCaml 4.10 or later (OCaml 5 not yet supported) due to an upstream constraint in `ocaml-rs`.

## Capabilities[​](#capabilities "Direct link to Capabilities")

* **Blocking API** — all operations are synchronous; no async runtime required.
* **Full result-type error handling** — every fallible function returns`('a, string) result`; no exceptions for storage errors.
* **Streaming readers and writers** — `Operator.reader` / `Operator.writer` for data that does not fit in memory.
* **Directory listing** — batch (`Operator.list`) and streaming (`Operator.lister` \+ `Operator.Lister.next`) variants.
* **Capability introspection** — check what a backend supports at runtime via`Operator.info` and the `Operator.Capability` module.
* **Services**: every backend the Rust core supports is available; the binding is built against the full Rust crate.

## Building from source[​](#building-from-source "Direct link to Building from source")

The binding has no opam release yet. Install the toolchain, then build with dune:

```

# 1. Install opam (if not already installed)

bash -c "sh <(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ocaml/opam/master/shell/install.sh)"


# 2. Create a 4.x switch (OCaml 5 is not supported)

opam switch create opendal-ocaml4.14 ocaml-base-compiler.4.14.0

eval $(opam env)


# 3. Install development tools (optional but recommended)

opam install -y utop odoc ounit2 ocaml-lsp-server ocamlformat ocamlformat-rpc


# 4. Build

cd bindings/ocaml

dune build


# 5. Run tests

dune test


```

You also need a working Rust toolchain (stable) for the `build.rs` step that compiles the Rust core into a native library.

## Useful Links[​](#useful-links "Direct link to Useful Links")

* **Source**: [bindings/ocaml/](https://github.com/apache/opendal/tree/main/bindings/ocaml)
* **Services & configuration**: [/services](/services/)
* **OCaml module reference**: `lib/operator.mli` in the source tree

## Next steps[​](#next-steps "Direct link to Next steps")

1. [Getting started](/docs/bindings/ocaml/getting-started/) — create an operator, read, write, and handle errors.

---

---
url: https://opendal.apache.org/docs/bindings/php/
---
# PHP | Apache OpenDAL™

# Apache OpenDAL™ PHP Binding

A PHP extension for OpenDAL: access S3, GCS, Azure Blob, the local filesystem, and [50+ more services](/services/) through one synchronous API, with the performance of the Rust core underneath.

New to the model behind the API? Read [Concepts](/docs/concepts/) first — service, operator, layer, and operation are the same four ideas in every language.

## Status[​](#status "Direct link to Status")

**Experimental / work in progress.** The binding is unreleased and has no published package. It is built from source as a native PHP extension using[ext-php-rs](https://github.com/davidcole1340/ext-php-rs). The API may change without notice. It is not ready for production use.

## Capabilities[​](#capabilities "Direct link to Capabilities")

* **Synchronous API** — all calls block; there is no async variant.
* **String and binary writes** — `write` for UTF-8 strings, `write_binary` for arbitrary byte arrays.
* **Metadata** — `stat` returns content length, content type, ETag, MD5, and entry mode (file or directory).
* **Directory operations** — `create_dir` creates directories recursively.
* **All services** that OpenDAL supports are available as long as they are enabled at compile time; see [Services](/services/).

## Building and installing the extension[​](#building-and-installing-the-extension "Direct link to Building and installing the extension")

The extension cannot be installed with `pecl` or through Composer. You must build it from the repository source.

**Requirements**

* PHP 8.1+
* Rust and Cargo — see [rust-lang.org/tools/install](https://www.rust-lang.org/tools/install)

**Step 1 — Clone the repository**

```

git clone https://github.com/apache/opendal.git


```

**Step 2 — Build the extension**

```

cd opendal/bindings/php

cargo build


```

Add `--release` for production builds.

**Step 3 — Enable the extension**

Copy the compiled library to PHP's extension directory and add the `extension=`directive to `php.ini`:

```

# Linux

cp target/debug/libopendal_php.so $(php -r "echo ini_get('extension_dir');")/libopendal_php.so

echo "extension=libopendal_php.so" >> $(php -r "echo php_ini_loaded_file();")


# macOS

cp target/debug/libopendal_php.dylib $(php -r "echo ini_get('extension_dir');")/libopendal_php.dylib

echo "extension=libopendal_php.dylib" >> $(php -r "echo php_ini_loaded_file();")


# Windows

cp target/debug/libopendal_php.dll $(php -r "echo ini_get('extension_dir');")/libopendal_php.dll

echo "extension=libopendal_php.dll" >> $(php -r "echo php_ini_loaded_file();")


```

Alternatively, use `cargo-php` to build and install in one step:

```

cargo install cargo-php

cd opendal/bindings/php

cargo php install


```

**Verify the installation**

```

php -m | grep opendal-php


```

## Useful Links[​](#useful-links "Direct link to Useful Links")

* **Source & examples**: [bindings/php/](https://github.com/apache/opendal/tree/main/bindings/php)
* **Services & configuration**: [/services](/services/)
* **ext-php-rs**: [github.com/davidcole1340/ext-php-rs](https://github.com/davidcole1340/ext-php-rs)

## Next steps[​](#next-steps "Direct link to Next steps")

1. [Getting started](/docs/bindings/php/getting-started/) — construct an operator, then write, read, inspect, and delete a file.

url: https://opendal.apache.org/docs/bindings/python/

# Ruby | Apache OpenDAL™

# Apache OpenDAL™ Ruby Binding

A native Ruby binding for OpenDAL: access S3, GCS, Azure Blob, the local filesystem, and [50+ more services](/services/) through one API, with the performance of the Rust core underneath.

New to the model behind the API? Read [Concepts](/docs/concepts/) first — service, operator, layer, operation are the same four ideas in every language.

note

This binding has its own version number, independent of the Rust core. Check compatibility against the binding's version, not the core's.

## Status[​](#status "Direct link to Status")

Released and published to [RubyGems](https://rubygems.org/gems/opendal).

## Capabilities[​](#capabilities "Direct link to Capabilities")

* **Synchronous** — every operation runs on the calling thread; there is no async API.
* **One API for every service** — the gem bundles all backends, selected by a scheme string at construction time.
* **File-like `IO`** — `op.open(path, "r")` returns a familiar read/write/seek/`readline` object you can stream through.
* **Middlewares** (OpenDAL layers) for retry, concurrency limits, throttling, and timeouts — see [Going to production](/docs/bindings/ruby/production/).

## Installation[​](#installation "Direct link to Installation")

```

gem install opendal


```

```

# Gemfile

gem "opendal"
```

# Swift | Apache OpenDAL™

# Apache OpenDAL™ Swift Binding

A Swift binding for OpenDAL: access storage services through one API, backed by the Rust core.

New to the model behind the API? Read [Concepts](/docs/concepts/) first — service, operator, and operation are the same ideas in every language.

Experimental / WIP

This binding is unreleased and under active development. The API surface is minimal, breaking changes may happen without notice, and it is **not suitable for production use**. Contributions welcome.

## Status[​](#status "Direct link to Status")

**Experimental — unreleased.** Only blocking read and write are implemented. The binding depends on the C binding and must be built from source; it is not published to any package registry.

## Capabilities[​](#capabilities "Direct link to Capabilities")

| Capability       | Status    |
| ---------------- | --------- |
| Blocking read    | Supported |
| Blocking write   | Supported |
| Async read/write | Not yet   |
| Stat / metadata  | Not yet   |
| List             | Not yet   |
| Delete           | Not yet   |
| Presigned URLs   | Not yet   |

The `Operator` class is the single entry point. It accepts a service scheme string and a dictionary of string options, matching the same model as every other OpenDAL binding. See [Services](/services/) for the full list of backends and their configuration keys.

## Installation[​](#installation "Direct link to Installation")

The package is **not published to any registry**. You must build the C dependency and reference the package by local path.

### 1\. Build the C dependency[​](#1-build-the-c-dependency "Direct link to 1. Build the C dependency")

```

cd bindings/swift

make build-c


```

### 2\. Add a local path dependency[​](#2-add-a-local-path-dependency "Direct link to 2. Add a local path dependency")

In your project's `Package.swift`:

```

// swift-tools-version:5.7

import PackageDescription


let package = Package(

    name: "MyTool",

    dependencies: [

        .package(path: "/path/to/opendal/bindings/swift/OpenDAL"),

    ],

    targets: [

        .target(name: "MyTool", dependencies: [

            .product(name: "OpenDAL", package: "OpenDAL"),

        ]),

    ]

)


```

Replace `/path/to/opendal` with the absolute path to your local checkout.

## Useful Links[​](#useful-links "Direct link to Useful Links")

* **User guide**: [opendal.apache.org/docs/bindings/swift](https://opendal.apache.org/docs/bindings/swift)
* **Services & configuration**: [/services](/services/)
* **Source**: [bindings/swift/](https://github.com/apache/opendal/tree/main/bindings/swift)
* **Contributing**: [bindings/swift/CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/apache/opendal/blob/main/bindings/swift/CONTRIBUTING.md)

## Next steps[​](#next-steps "Direct link to Next steps")

1. [Getting started](/docs/bindings/swift/getting-started/) — create an operator, write data, read it back.

# Zig | Apache OpenDAL™

# Apache OpenDAL™ Zig Binding

A Zig binding for OpenDAL, giving Zig programs blocking access to S3, GCS, Azure Blob, the local filesystem, and [50+ more services](/services/) through one API backed by the Rust core.

New to the model behind the API? Read [Concepts](/docs/concepts/) first — service, operator, layer, operation are the same four ideas in every language.

Early-stage / Experimental

This binding is **early-stage and a work in progress**. The API is incomplete, there are no pre-built packages, and breaking changes may occur. It must be built from source. It is not yet published to any package registry.

## Status[​](#status "Direct link to Status")

Unreleased and experimental (version `0.0.1`). The Zig binding wraps the OpenDAL C binding (`libopendal_c`) using Zig's `translate-c` mechanism, then exposes a thin idiomatic Zig layer on top. Only synchronous (blocking) operations are implemented; an async interface using coroutines is noted as WIP in the source.

Requires Zig 0.14.0 or higher.

## Capabilities[​](#capabilities "Direct link to Capabilities")

Operations available through `Operator`:

* **write** — write bytes to a path
* **read** — read bytes from a path
* **stat** — fetch metadata (size, type, content-type, etag, last-modified)
* **delete** — remove a path
* **exists** — check whether a path exists
* **list** — iterate directory entries (returns a `Lister` yielding `Entry` values)
* **createDir** — create a directory path
* **rename** — rename/move a path (supported only on backends that expose this capability)
* **copy** — copy a path (supported only on backends that expose this capability)

All operations return Zig errors (`OpendalError`) rather than raw C error codes.

`info()` / `OperatorInfo` exist in the source but are currently broken (a compile error in `src/opendal.zig` references an undefined variable) and cannot be used until the bug is fixed.

The binding also exposes the raw C API under the `opendal.c` namespace for cases where the thin layer does not yet cover what you need.

## Building[​](#building "Direct link to Building")

Requirements: Zig 0.14.0 or higher, a C/C++ compiler, CMake, and a Rust toolchain.

```

# From bindings/zig/


# 1. Build the underlying C library (runs cmake + make under bindings/c)

zig build libopendal_c


# 2. Build and run the unit tests

zig build test --summary all


```

The C library lands in `bindings/c/target/debug/` (or `release/` with`-Doptimize=ReleaseSafe`). The header is at `bindings/c/include/opendal.h`.

To build in release mode:

```

zig build libopendal_c -Doptimize=ReleaseSafe

zig build test -Doptimize=ReleaseSafe --summary all


```

## Using the module in your project[​](#using-the-module-in-your-project "Direct link to Using the module in your project")

Add the module to your `build.zig`:

```

const opendal_dep = b.dependency("opendal", .{

    .target = target,

    .optimize = optimize,

});

exe.root_module.addImport("opendal", opendal_dep.module("opendal"));


```

Then import it:

```

const opendal = @import("opendal");


```

## Useful Links[​](#useful-links "Direct link to Useful Links")

* **Source**: [bindings/zig/](https://github.com/apache/opendal/tree/main/bindings/zig)
* **Services & configuration**: [/services](/services/)
* **C binding (underlying layer)**: [bindings/c/](https://github.com/apache/opendal/tree/main/bindings/c)
* **Contributing**: [bindings/zig/CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/apache/opendal/blob/main/bindings/zig/CONTRIBUTING.md)

## Next steps[​](#next-steps "Direct link to Next steps")

1. [Getting started](/docs/bindings/zig/getting-started/) — build an operator, write and read data, inspect metadata, and list a directory.

# Connecting to your storage | Apache OpenDAL™

# Connecting to your storage

Switching backends is a configuration change, not a code change. This page covers the ways to build an operator and the things that trip people up: feature flags and credentials. For the configuration keys of a specific service, see [Services](/services/).

## Enable the service feature[​](#enable-the-service-feature "Direct link to Enable the service feature")

Every service is behind a `services-*` Cargo feature. Enable the ones you use:

```

[dependencies]

opendal = { version = "0.57", features = ["services-s3", "services-gcs"] }


```

The in-memory service is always available; every other service needs its`services-*` feature. Forgetting the feature is the most common reason a service "doesn't exist" at compile time.

## Build an operator[​](#build-an-operator "Direct link to Build an operator")

### From a typed builder (recommended)[​](#from-a-typed-builder-recommended "Direct link to From a typed builder (recommended)")

Each service has a builder with checked, discoverable options:

```

use opendal::services;

use opendal::Operator;


let op = Operator::new(

    services::S3::default()

        .bucket("my-bucket")

        .region("us-east-1")

        .endpoint("https://s3.amazonaws.com"),

)?;


```

A local filesystem operator roots every path under one directory:

```

let op = Operator::new(services::Fs::default().root("/tmp/opendal"))?;


```

### From a key-value map[​](#from-a-key-value-map "Direct link to From a key-value map")

When configuration comes from a file or environment, build from key-value pairs instead of naming each setter. The keys are the same as the builder fields:

```

use std::collections::HashMap;

use opendal::services;

use opendal::Operator;


let cfg = HashMap::from([

    ("bucket".to_string(), "my-bucket".to_string()),

    ("region".to_string(), "us-east-1".to_string()),

]);


let op = Operator::from_iter::<services::S3>(cfg)?;


```

### From a URI[​](#from-a-uri "Direct link to From a URI")

Registered services can be constructed from a URI, with the scheme selecting the backend at runtime:

```

use opendal::Operator;


let op = Operator::from_uri("memory://")?;


```

Use `Operator::via_iter(scheme, pairs)` for the same runtime-selected construction with explicit key-value config.

## Credentials[​](#credentials "Direct link to Credentials")

Credentials are just more configuration keys. Set them on the builder (for example S3's `access_key_id` / `secret_access_key`), or supply them through the key-value map above. Some services can also load credentials from their platform's default sources — see each service's page under [Services](/services/)for the exact keys and credential behavior.

Avoid hard-coding secrets in source. Read them from the environment or a secret manager and pass them in when you build the operator.

## One operator per service and root[​](#one-operator-per-service-and-root "Direct link to One operator per service and root")

An operator maps to one service and one root path. To work with two buckets or two roots, build two operators — they are cheap, lightweight handles that are safe to share across threads and tasks.

---

---
url: https://opendal.apache.org/docs/core/getting-started/
---
# Getting started | Apache OpenDAL™

# Getting started

## Your first program[​](#your-first-program "Direct link to Your first program")

This program builds an operator, writes a file, reads it back, inspects its metadata, and deletes it. It runs against the in-memory service with no credentials, so you can paste it into a fresh project and run it as-is.

```

# Cargo.toml

[dependencies]

opendal = "0.57"

tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }


```

```

use opendal::Operator;

use opendal::Result;

use opendal::services;


#[tokio::main]

async fn main() -> Result<()> {

    // Configure a service, then build an operator from it.

    let op = Operator::new(services::Memory::default())?;


    // The same verbs work on every service.

    op.write("hello.txt", "Hello, World!").await?;


    let bytes = op.read("hello.txt").await?;

    println!("read {} bytes", bytes.len());


    let meta = op.stat("hello.txt").await?;

    println!("size = {} bytes", meta.content_length());


    op.delete("hello.txt").await?;

    Ok(())

}


```

## Point it at a real backend[​](#point-it-at-a-real-backend "Direct link to Point it at a real backend")

Only the service changes; the operations stay identical. To use S3, enable its feature and configure the builder:

```

opendal = { version = "0.57", features = ["services-s3"] }


```

```

use opendal::services;

use opendal::Operator;


let op = Operator::new(

    services::S3::default()

        .bucket("my-bucket")

        .region("us-east-1"),

)?;


op.write("hello.txt", "Hello from S3!").await?;


```

Each service is gated behind its own `services-*` feature, so enable the ones you use. The next page, [Connecting to your storage](/docs/core/connecting/), covers the construction options and credentials in depth; [Services](/services/) lists every backend and its configuration keys.

## Blocking vs async[​](#blocking-vs-async "Direct link to Blocking vs async")

OpenDAL is async-first. [Operator](https://docs.rs/opendal/latest/opendal/struct.Operator.html) exposes `async` methods and needs an async runtime (Tokio by default).

For synchronous code, enable the `blocking` feature and use[blocking::Operator](https://docs.rs/opendal/latest/opendal/blocking/struct.Operator.html). Build it from an async operator, then call the same verbs without `.await`:

```

opendal = { version = "0.57", features = ["services-memory", "blocking"] }


```

```

use opendal::blocking;

use opendal::services;

use opendal::Operator;


// Build the async operator first, outside any async runtime.

let op = Operator::new(services::Memory::default())?;

let bop = blocking::Operator::new(op)?;


bop.write("hello.txt", "Hello, World!")?;

let bytes = bop.read("hello.txt")?;


```

The blocking operator drives the async one on a managed runtime, so construct it outside of an existing async context.

---

---
url: https://opendal.apache.org/docs/core/production/
---
# Going to production | Apache OpenDAL™

# Going to production

The basics read and write data. Production code also has to survive transient failures, bound its resource use, observe what happens, and react to errors. OpenDAL handles the first three with **layers** and gives you typed **errors**and **capability checks** for the rest.

## Layers[​](#layers "Direct link to Layers")

A layer wraps an operator to add cross-cutting behavior without touching your storage code. Apply one with `.layer()`; it returns a new operator. Layers nest like an onion, and the **last** layer applied is the **outermost** — it sees the request first and the response last. A robust order is logging on the outside, retry on the inside, so each retry attempt is logged:

```

use std::time::Duration;

use opendal::layers::{ConcurrentLimitLayer, LoggingLayer, RetryLayer, TimeoutLayer};


let op = Operator::new(builder)?

    .layer(RetryLayer::new().with_max_times(3))      // retry transient failures

    .layer(TimeoutLayer::new().with_timeout(Duration::from_secs(10))) // bound slow calls

    .layer(ConcurrentLimitLayer::new(16))            // cap in-flight requests

    .layer(LoggingLayer::default());                 // log every attempt (outermost)


```

These four ship in the default features. See [Concepts](/docs/concepts/#layer)for the model.

## Observability[​](#observability "Direct link to Observability")

`LoggingLayer` (above) emits logs through the `log` crate. For metrics and tracing, enable the matching feature and apply the layer the same way:

| Feature           | Layer           | Use it for                             |
| ----------------- | --------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| layers-metrics    | MetricsLayer    | metrics\-crate counters and histograms |
| layers-prometheus | PrometheusLayer | Prometheus metrics                     |
| layers-tracing    | TracingLayer    | tracing spans                          |
| layers-fastrace   | FastraceLayer   | distributed tracing with fastrace      |
| layers-otel-trace | OtelTraceLayer  | OpenTelemetry traces                   |

See the [layers module](https://docs.rs/opendal/latest/opendal/layers/index.html) for the full list and each layer's options.

## Error handling[​](#error-handling "Direct link to Error handling")

Every fallible call returns `opendal::Result<T>`, an alias for`Result<T, opendal::Error>`. Match on [ErrorKind](https://docs.rs/opendal/latest/opendal/enum.ErrorKind.html) to branch on what went wrong instead of parsing messages:

```

use opendal::ErrorKind;


match op.read("maybe-missing.txt").await {

    Ok(bytes) => { /* ... */ }

    Err(err) if err.kind() == ErrorKind::NotFound => { /* handle absence */ }

    Err(err) => return Err(err),

}


```

Common kinds include `NotFound`, `PermissionDenied`, `AlreadyExists`,`RateLimited`, and `Unsupported`. Note that `delete` is idempotent — deleting a missing path succeeds rather than returning `NotFound`.

## Capability checks[​](#capability-checks "Direct link to Capability checks")

Not every service supports every operation. Query what a backend can do with[Capability](https://docs.rs/opendal/latest/opendal/struct.Capability.html) before calling optional operations like `copy`, `rename`, or presign:

```

let cap = op.info().capability();

if cap.copy {

    op.copy("a.txt", "b.txt").await?;

}


```

Calling an unsupported operation returns an error with `ErrorKind::Unsupported`, so capability checks are an optimization, not a requirement for safety.

---

---
url: https://opendal.apache.org/docs/core/tasks/
---
# Common tasks | Apache OpenDAL™

# Common tasks

Recipes for the things you actually do with storage. Each works on every service. They assume an `op: Operator` built as in[Getting started](/docs/core/getting-started/). For full method signatures and options, follow the links to the [API reference](https://docs.rs/opendal/latest/opendal/struct.Operator.html).

A few conventions used throughout:

* Paths are relative to the operator's root; a trailing `/` means a directory (`logs/app/` is a directory, `logs/app` is a file).
* Reads return a [Buffer](https://docs.rs/opendal/latest/opendal/struct.Buffer.html); writes accept anything that converts into one (`&str`, `String`, `Vec<u8>`, `Bytes`).
* Most verbs have a `*_with` companion (`read_with`, `write_with`, …) for extra options like ranges, content type, and concurrency.

## Read a whole file[​](#read-a-whole-file "Direct link to Read a whole file")

```

let bytes = op.read("path/to/file").await?;

let text = String::from_utf8(bytes.to_vec())?;


```

## Read part of a file[​](#read-part-of-a-file "Direct link to Read part of a file")

```

let bytes = op.read_with("path/to/file").range(0..1024).await?;


```

## Stream a large file[​](#stream-a-large-file "Direct link to Stream a large file")

Don't buffer gigabytes in memory — stream chunks instead:

```

use futures::TryStreamExt;


let mut stream = op.reader("big.bin").await?.into_bytes_stream(..).await?;

while let Some(chunk) = stream.try_next().await? {

    // process chunk (bytes::Bytes)

}


```

## Write a whole file[​](#write-a-whole-file "Direct link to Write a whole file")

```

let _meta = op.write("path/to/file", "Hello, World!").await?;

let _meta = op.write("path/to/file", vec![0u8; 1024]).await?;


```

## Stream a large upload[​](#stream-a-large-upload "Direct link to Stream a large upload")

Use a [Writer](https://docs.rs/opendal/latest/opendal/struct.Writer.html) for data produced incrementally. Call `write` repeatedly, then`close` to commit (use `abort` to discard):

```

let mut writer = op.writer("big.bin").await?;

writer.write(first_chunk).await?;

writer.write(second_chunk).await?;

let _meta = writer.close().await?;


```

## Upload concurrently[​](#upload-concurrently "Direct link to Upload concurrently")

For large objects on services with multipart support, upload parts in parallel:

```

let mut writer = op.writer_with("big.bin").concurrent(8).await?;

writer.write(data).await?;

writer.close().await?;


```

## Check existence and metadata[​](#check-existence-and-metadata "Direct link to Check existence and metadata")

```

if op.exists("path/to/file").await? {

    let meta = op.stat("path/to/file").await?;

    println!("{} bytes, dir = {}", meta.content_length(), meta.is_dir());

}


```

## List a directory[​](#list-a-directory "Direct link to List a directory")

`list` returns the direct children of a directory:

```

for entry in op.list("dir/").await? {

    println!("{} ({:?})", entry.path(), entry.metadata().mode());

}


```

For large directories, stream entries with a [Lister](https://docs.rs/opendal/latest/opendal/struct.Lister.html) instead of collecting them into a `Vec`:

```

use futures::TryStreamExt;


let mut lister = op.lister("dir/").await?;

while let Some(entry) = lister.try_next().await? {

    println!("{}", entry.path());

}


```

## Walk a tree recursively[​](#walk-a-tree-recursively "Direct link to Walk a tree recursively")

```

let entries = op.list_with("dir/").recursive(true).await?;


```

`lister_with("dir/").recursive(true)` gives the streaming equivalent.

## Delete a file or a whole tree[​](#delete-a-file-or-a-whole-tree "Direct link to Delete a file or a whole tree")

```

op.delete("path/to/file").await?;   // single path; idempotent

op.remove_all("dir/").await?;       // a path and everything under it


```

`delete` succeeds even if the path does not exist.

## Create a directory[​](#create-a-directory "Direct link to Create a directory")

```

op.create_dir("path/to/dir/").await?;   // the trailing slash is required


```

## Copy and rename[​](#copy-and-rename "Direct link to Copy and rename")

```

let _meta = op.copy("from.txt", "to.txt").await?;

op.rename("old.txt", "new.txt").await?;


```

Both operate within a single operator and require a service that supports them; see [capability checks](/docs/core/production/#capability-checks).

## Generate a presigned URL[​](#generate-a-presigned-url "Direct link to Generate a presigned URL")

Hand a time-limited URL to a third party so they can access an object without your credentials:

```

use std::time::Duration;


let req = op.presign_read("path/to/file", Duration::from_secs(3600)).await?;

// req.method(), req.uri(), req.header() describe the HTTP request to make


```

url: https://opendal.apache.org/services/aliyun-drive/go/

# aliyun-drive

6 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/aliyun-drive"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(aliyun-drive.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	"drive_type": "...",

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/aliyun-drive"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(aliyun-drive.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// The Root of this backend.

	//

	// All operations will happen under this root.

	//

	// Default to `/` if not set.

	// "root": "...",


	// The access_token of this backend.

	//

	// Solution for client-only purpose. #4733

	//

	// Required if no client_id, client_secret and refresh_token are provided.

	// "access_token": "...",


	// The client_id of this backend.

	//

	// Required if no access_token is provided.

	// "client_id": "...",


	// The client_secret of this backend.

	//

	// Required if no access_token is provided.

	// "client_secret": "...",


	// The refresh_token of this backend.

	//

	// Required if no access_token is provided.

	// "refresh_token": "...",


	// The drive_type of this backend.

	//

	// All operations will happen under this type of drive.

	//

	// Available values are `default`, `backup` and `resource`.

	//

	// Fallback to default if not set or no other drives can be found.

	"drive_type": "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General6

| Key            | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| -------------- | ------ | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| root           | string | no       | The Root of this backend. All operations will happen under this root. Default to / if not set.                                                                                                           |
| access\_token  | string | no       | The access\_token of this backend. Solution for client-only purpose. #4733 Required if no client\_id, client\_secret and refresh\_token are provided.                                                    |
| client\_id     | string | no       | The client\_id of this backend. Required if no access\_token is provided.                                                                                                                                |
| client\_secret | string | no       | The client\_secret of this backend. Required if no access\_token is provided.                                                                                                                            |
| refresh\_token | string | no       | The refresh\_token of this backend. Required if no access\_token is provided.                                                                                                                            |
| drive\_type    | string | yes      | The drive\_type of this backend. All operations will happen under this type of drive. Available values are default, backup and resource. Fallback to default if not set or no other drives can be found. |

# aliyun-drive

6 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

import java.util.HashMap;

import java.util.Map;

import org.apache.opendal.Operator;


Map<String, String> config = new HashMap<>();

config.put("drive_type", "...");

Operator operator = Operator.of("aliyun-drive", config);
```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import java.util.HashMap;

import java.util.Map;

import org.apache.opendal.Operator;


Map<String, String> config = new HashMap<>();

// The Root of this backend.

//

// All operations will happen under this root.

//

// Default to `/` if not set.

// config.put("root", "...");


// The access_token of this backend.

//

// Solution for client-only purpose. #4733

//

// Required if no client_id, client_secret and refresh_token are provided.

// config.put("access_token", "...");


// The client_id of this backend.

//

// Required if no access_token is provided.

// config.put("client_id", "...");


// The client_secret of this backend.

//

// Required if no access_token is provided.

// config.put("client_secret", "...");


// The refresh_token of this backend.

//

// Required if no access_token is provided.

// config.put("refresh_token", "...");


// The drive_type of this backend.

//

// All operations will happen under this type of drive.

//

// Available values are `default`, `backup` and `resource`.

//

// Fallback to default if not set or no other drives can be found.

config.put("drive_type", "...");

Operator operator = Operator.of("aliyun-drive", config);


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General6

| Key            | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| -------------- | ------ | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| root           | string | no       | The Root of this backend. All operations will happen under this root. Default to / if not set.                                                                                                           |
| access\_token  | string | no       | The access\_token of this backend. Solution for client-only purpose. #4733 Required if no client\_id, client\_secret and refresh\_token are provided.                                                    |
| client\_id     | string | no       | The client\_id of this backend. Required if no access\_token is provided.                                                                                                                                |
| client\_secret | string | no       | The client\_secret of this backend. Required if no access\_token is provided.                                                                                                                            |
| refresh\_token | string | no       | The refresh\_token of this backend. Required if no access\_token is provided.                                                                                                                            |
| drive\_type    | string | yes      | The drive\_type of this backend. All operations will happen under this type of drive. Available values are default, backup and resource. Fallback to default if not set or no other drives can be found. |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

# alluxio

2 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/alluxio"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(alluxio.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/alluxio"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(alluxio.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// root of this backend.

	//

	// All operations will happen under this root.

	//

	// default to `/` if not set.

	// "root": "...",


	// endpoint of this backend.

	//

	// Endpoint must be full uri, mostly like `http://127.0.0.1:39999`.

	// "endpoint": "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General2

| Key      | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                |
| -------- | ------ | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| root     | string | no       | root of this backend. All operations will happen under this root. default to / if not set. |
| endpoint | string | no       | endpoint of this backend. Endpoint must be full uri, mostly like http://127.0.0.1:39999.   |

[Services](/services/) / alluxio

# alluxio

2 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

use opendal::Operator;


let operator = Operator::via_iter("alluxio", [

])?;


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

use opendal::Operator;


let operator = Operator::via_iter("alluxio", [

    // root of this backend.

    //

    // All operations will happen under this root.

    //

    // default to `/` if not set.

    // ("root".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // endpoint of this backend.

    //

    // Endpoint must be full uri, mostly like `http://127.0.0.1:39999`.

    // ("endpoint".to_string(), "...".to_string()),

])?;


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General2

| Key      | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                |
| -------- | ------ | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| root     | string | no       | root of this backend. All operations will happen under this root. default to / if not set. |
| endpoint | string | no       | endpoint of this backend. Endpoint must be full uri, mostly like http://127.0.0.1:39999.   |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

Apache OpenDAL, OpenDAL, Apache, the Apache feather and the Apache OpenDAL project logo are either registered trademarks or trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.

[Services](/services/) / azblob

# azblob

11 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsRubyGo

Quick start

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/azblob"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(azblob.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	"container": "...",

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/azblob"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(azblob.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// The root of Azblob service backend.

	//

	// All operations will happen under this root.

	// "root": "...",


	// The container name of Azblob service backend.

	"container": "...",


	// The endpoint of Azblob service backend.

	//

	// Endpoint must be full uri, e.g.

	//

	// - Azblob: `https://accountname.blob.core.windows.net`

	// - Azurite: `http://127.0.0.1:10000/devstoreaccount1`

	// "endpoint": "...",


	// The account name of Azblob service backend.

	// "account_name": "...",


	// The account key of Azblob service backend.

	// "account_key": "...",


	// The encryption key of Azblob service backend.

	// "encryption_key": "...",


	// The encryption key sha256 of Azblob service backend.

	// "encryption_key_sha256": "...",


	// The encryption algorithm of Azblob service backend.

	// "encryption_algorithm": "...",


	// The sas token of Azblob service backend.

	// "sas_token": "...",


	// Skip signature will skip loading credentials and signing requests.

	// "skip_signature": "true",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General11

| Key                              | Type    | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| -------------------------------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| root                             | string  | no       | The root of Azblob service backend. All operations will happen under this root.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| container                        | string  | yes      | The container name of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| endpoint                         | string  | no       | The endpoint of Azblob service backend. Endpoint must be full uri, e.g. - Azblob: https://accountname.blob.core.windows.net\- Azurite: http://127.0.0.1:10000/devstoreaccount1                                                                                                                                            |
| account\_name                    | string  | no       | The account name of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| account\_key                     | string  | no       | The account key of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| encryption\_key                  | string  | no       | The encryption key of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| encryption\_key\_sha256          | string  | no       | The encryption key sha256 of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| encryption\_algorithm            | string  | no       | The encryption algorithm of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| sas\_token                       | string  | no       | The sas token of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| batch\_max\_operationsdeprecated | integer | no       | Deprecated: Azblob delete batch capability is enabled by default with Azure Blob's 256-operation batch limit.Deprecated since 0.57.0: Azblob delete batch capability is enabled by default with Azure Blob's 256-operation batch limit. Use CapabilityOverrideLayer to override delete\_max\_size for specific endpoints. |
| skip\_signature                  | bool    | no       | Skip signature will skip loading credentials and signing requests.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

Copyright © 2022-2026, The Apache Software Foundation  
Apache OpenDAL, OpenDAL, Apache, the Apache feather and the Apache OpenDAL project logo are either registered trademarks or trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.

# azblob

11 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsRubyGo

Quick start

```

import java.util.HashMap;

import java.util.Map;

import org.apache.opendal.Operator;


Map<String, String> config = new HashMap<>();

config.put("container", "...");

Operator operator = Operator.of("azblob", config);


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import java.util.HashMap;

import java.util.Map;

import org.apache.opendal.Operator;


Map<String, String> config = new HashMap<>();

// The root of Azblob service backend.

//

// All operations will happen under this root.

// config.put("root", "...");


// The container name of Azblob service backend.

config.put("container", "...");


// The endpoint of Azblob service backend.

//

// Endpoint must be full uri, e.g.

//

// - Azblob: `https://accountname.blob.core.windows.net`

// - Azurite: `http://127.0.0.1:10000/devstoreaccount1`

// config.put("endpoint", "...");


// The account name of Azblob service backend.

// config.put("account_name", "...");


// The account key of Azblob service backend.

// config.put("account_key", "...");


// The encryption key of Azblob service backend.

// config.put("encryption_key", "...");


// The encryption key sha256 of Azblob service backend.

// config.put("encryption_key_sha256", "...");


// The encryption algorithm of Azblob service backend.

// config.put("encryption_algorithm", "...");


// The sas token of Azblob service backend.

// config.put("sas_token", "...");


// Skip signature will skip loading credentials and signing requests.

// config.put("skip_signature", "true");

Operator operator = Operator.of("azblob", config);


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General11

| Key                              | Type    | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| -------------------------------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| root                             | string  | no       | The root of Azblob service backend. All operations will happen under this root.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| container                        | string  | yes      | The container name of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| endpoint                         | string  | no       | The endpoint of Azblob service backend. Endpoint must be full uri, e.g. - Azblob: https://accountname.blob.core.windows.net\- Azurite: http://127.0.0.1:10000/devstoreaccount1                                                                                                                                            |
| account\_name                    | string  | no       | The account name of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| account\_key                     | string  | no       | The account key of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| encryption\_key                  | string  | no       | The encryption key of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| encryption\_key\_sha256          | string  | no       | The encryption key sha256 of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| encryption\_algorithm            | string  | no       | The encryption algorithm of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| sas\_token                       | string  | no       | The sas token of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| batch\_max\_operationsdeprecated | integer | no       | Deprecated: Azblob delete batch capability is enabled by default with Azure Blob's 256-operation batch limit.Deprecated since 0.57.0: Azblob delete batch capability is enabled by default with Azure Blob's 256-operation batch limit. Use CapabilityOverrideLayer to override delete\_max\_size for specific endpoints. |
| skip\_signature                  | bool    | no       | Skip signature will skip loading credentials and signing requests.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |

# azblob

11 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsRubyGo

Quick start

```

import { Operator } from "opendal";


const operator = new Operator("azblob", {

  container: "...",

});


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import { Operator } from "opendal";


const operator = new Operator("azblob", {

  // The root of Azblob service backend.

  //

  // All operations will happen under this root.

  // root: "...",


  // The container name of Azblob service backend.

  container: "...",


  // The endpoint of Azblob service backend.

  //

  // Endpoint must be full uri, e.g.

  //

  // - Azblob: `https://accountname.blob.core.windows.net`

  // - Azurite: `http://127.0.0.1:10000/devstoreaccount1`

  // endpoint: "...",


  // The account name of Azblob service backend.

  // account_name: "...",


  // The account key of Azblob service backend.

  // account_key: "...",


  // The encryption key of Azblob service backend.

  // encryption_key: "...",


  // The encryption key sha256 of Azblob service backend.

  // encryption_key_sha256: "...",


  // The encryption algorithm of Azblob service backend.

  // encryption_algorithm: "...",


  // The sas token of Azblob service backend.

  // sas_token: "...",


  // Skip signature will skip loading credentials and signing requests.

  // skip_signature: "true",

});


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General11

| Key                              | Type    | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| -------------------------------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| root                             | string  | no       | The root of Azblob service backend. All operations will happen under this root.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| container                        | string  | yes      | The container name of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| endpoint                         | string  | no       | The endpoint of Azblob service backend. Endpoint must be full uri, e.g. - Azblob: https://accountname.blob.core.windows.net\- Azurite: http://127.0.0.1:10000/devstoreaccount1                                                                                                                                            |
| account\_name                    | string  | no       | The account name of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| account\_key                     | string  | no       | The account key of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| encryption\_key                  | string  | no       | The encryption key of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| encryption\_key\_sha256          | string  | no       | The encryption key sha256 of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| encryption\_algorithm            | string  | no       | The encryption algorithm of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| sas\_token                       | string  | no       | The sas token of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| batch\_max\_operationsdeprecated | integer | no       | Deprecated: Azblob delete batch capability is enabled by default with Azure Blob's 256-operation batch limit.Deprecated since 0.57.0: Azblob delete batch capability is enabled by default with Azure Blob's 256-operation batch limit. Use CapabilityOverrideLayer to override delete\_max\_size for specific endpoints. |
| skip\_signature                  | bool    | no       | Skip signature will skip loading credentials and signing requests.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |

# azblob

11 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsRubyGo

Quick start

```

import opendal


operator = opendal.Operator(

    "azblob",

    container="...",

)


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import opendal


operator = opendal.Operator(

    "azblob",

    # The root of Azblob service backend.

    #

    # All operations will happen under this root.

    # root="...",


    # The container name of Azblob service backend.

    container="...",


    # The endpoint of Azblob service backend.

    #

    # Endpoint must be full uri, e.g.

    #

    # - Azblob: `https://accountname.blob.core.windows.net`

    # - Azurite: `http://127.0.0.1:10000/devstoreaccount1`

    # endpoint="...",


    # The account name of Azblob service backend.

    # account_name="...",


    # The account key of Azblob service backend.

    # account_key="...",


    # The encryption key of Azblob service backend.

    # encryption_key="...",


    # The encryption key sha256 of Azblob service backend.

    # encryption_key_sha256="...",


    # The encryption algorithm of Azblob service backend.

    # encryption_algorithm="...",


    # The sas token of Azblob service backend.

    # sas_token="...",


    # Skip signature will skip loading credentials and signing requests.

    # skip_signature="true",

)


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

| root                             | string  | no       | The root of Azblob service backend. All operations will happen under this root.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| container                        | string  | yes      | The container name of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| endpoint                         | string  | no       | The endpoint of Azblob service backend. | account\_name                    | string  | no       | The account name of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |

| encryption\_key                  | string  | no       | The encryption key of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |

```
require "opendal"


operator = OpenDal::Operator.new("azblob", {

  # The root of Azblob service backend.

  #

  # All operations will happen under this root.

  # "root" => "...",


  # The container name of Azblob service backend.

  "container" => "...",


  # The endpoint of Azblob service backend.

  # - Azblob: `https://accountname.blob.core.windows.net`

  # - Azurite: `http://127.0.0.1:10000/devstoreaccount1`

  # "endpoint" => "...",


  # The account name of Azblob service backend.

  # "account_name" => "...",


  # The account key of Azblob service backend.

  # "account_key" => "...",


  # The encryption key of Azblob service backend.

  # "encryption_key" => "...",


  # The encryption key sha256 of Azblob service backend.

  # "encryption_key_sha256" => "...",


  # The encryption algorithm of Azblob service backend.

  # "encryption_algorithm" => "...",


  # The sas token of Azblob service backend.

  # "sas_token" => "...",


  # Skip signature will skip loading credentials and signing requests.

  # "skip_signature" => "true",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

| container                        | string  | yes      | The container name of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| endpoint                         | string  | no       | The endpoint of Azblob service backend. Endpoint must be full uri, e.g. - Azblob: https://accountname.blob.core.windows.net\- Azurite: http://127.0.0.1:10000/devstoreaccount1                                                                                                                                            |
| account\_name                    | string  | no       | The account name of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| account\_key                     | string  | no       | The account key of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| encryption\_key                  | string  | no       | The encryption key of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| encryption\_key\_sha256          | string  | no       | The encryption key sha256 of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| encryption\_algorithm            | string  | no       | The encryption algorithm of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| sas\_token                       | string  | no       | The sas token of Azblob service backend.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| batch\_max\_operationsdeprecated | integer | no       | Deprecated: Azblob delete batch capability is enabled by default with Azure Blob's 256-operation batch limit.Deprecated since 0.57.0: Azblob delete batch capability is enabled by default with Azure Blob's 256-operation batch limit. Use CapabilityOverrideLayer to override delete\_max\_size for specific endpoints. |
| skip\_signature                  | bool    | no       | Skip signature will skip loading credentials and signing requests.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)
