# foundationdb

2 configuration options · available in Rust, Python, Node.js, Go

RustPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/foundationdb"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(foundationdb.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/foundationdb"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(foundationdb.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// root of the backend.

	// "root": "...",


	// config_path for the backend.

	// "config_path": "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General2

| Key          | Type   | Required | Description                   |
| ------------ | ------ | -------- | ----------------------------- |
| root         | string | no       | root of the backend.          |
| config\_path | string | no       | config\_path for the backend. |

```

use opendal::Operator;


let operator = Operator::via_iter("foundationdb", [

    // root of the backend.

    // ("root".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // config_path for the backend.

    // ("config_path".to_string(), "...".to_string()),

])?;


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General2

| Key          | Type   | Required | Description                   |
| ------------ | ------ | -------- | ----------------------------- |
| root         | string | no       | root of the backend.          |
| config\_path | string | no       | config\_path for the backend. |

# foyer

8 configuration options · available in Rust, Python, Go

RustPythonGo

Quick start

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/foyer"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(foyer.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/foyer"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(foyer.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// Name for this cache instance.

	// "name": "...",


	// Root path of this backend.

	// "root": "...",


	// Memory capacity in bytes for the cache.

	// "memory": "1000",


	// Disk cache directory path.

	//

	// If set, enables hybrid cache with disk storage. Data will be persisted to

	// this directory when memory cache is full.

	// "disk_path": "...",


	// Disk cache total capacity in bytes.

	// Only used when `disk_path` is set.

	// "disk_capacity": "1000",


	// Individual cache file size in bytes.

	//

	// Default is 1 MiB.

	// Only used when `disk_path` is set.

	// "disk_file_size": "1000",


	// Recovery mode when starting the cache.

	//

	// Valid values: "none" (default), "quiet", "strict".

	// - "none": Don't recover from disk

	// - "quiet": Recover and skip errors

	// - "strict": Recover and panic on errors

	// "recover_mode": "...",


	// Number of shards for concurrent access.

	//

	// Default is 1. Higher values improve concurrency but increase overhead.

	// "shards": "1000",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General8

| Key              | Type    | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| ---------------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| name             | string  | no       | Name for this cache instance.                                                                                                                                                                          |
| root             | string  | no       | Root path of this backend.                                                                                                                                                                             |
| memory           | integer | no       | Memory capacity in bytes for the cache.                                                                                                                                                                |
| disk\_path       | string  | no       | Disk cache directory path. If set, enables hybrid cache with disk storage. Data will be persisted to this directory when memory cache is full.                                                         |
| disk\_capacity   | integer | no       | Disk cache total capacity in bytes. Only used when disk\_path is set.                                                                                                                                  |
| disk\_file\_size | integer | no       | Individual cache file size in bytes. Default is 1 MiB. Only used when disk\_path is set.                                                                                                               |
| recover\_mode    | string  | no       | Recovery mode when starting the cache. Valid values: "none" (default), "quiet", "strict". - "none": Don't recover from disk - "quiet": Recover and skip errors - "strict": Recover and panic on errors |
| shards           | integer | no       | Number of shards for concurrent access. Default is 1\. Higher values improve concurrency but increase overhead.                                                                                        |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

Apache OpenDAL, OpenDAL, Apache, the Apache feather and the Apache OpenDAL project logo are either registered trademarks or trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.

# fs

2 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsRubyGo

Quick start

```

use opendal::Operator;


let operator = Operator::via_iter("fs", [

])?;


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

use opendal::Operator;


let operator = Operator::via_iter("fs", [

    // root dir for backend

    // ("root".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // tmp dir for atomic write

    // ("atomic_write_dir".to_string(), "...".to_string()),

])?;


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General2

| Key                | Type   | Required | Description              |
| ------------------ | ------ | -------- | ------------------------ |
| root               | string | no       | root dir for backend     |
| atomic\_write\_dir | string | no       | tmp dir for atomic write |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

# ftp

4 configuration options · available in Rust, Python, Node.js, Go

RustPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/ftp"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(ftp.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/ftp"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(ftp.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// endpoint of this backend

	// "endpoint": "...",


	// root of this backend

	// "root": "...",


	// user of this backend

	// "user": "...",


	// password of this backend

	// "password": "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General4

| Key      | Type   | Required | Description              |
| -------- | ------ | -------- | ------------------------ |
| endpoint | string | no       | endpoint of this backend |
| root     | string | no       | root of this backend     |
| user     | string | no       | user of this backend     |
| password | string | no       | password of this backend |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

# ftp

4 configuration options · available in Rust, Python, Node.js, Go

RustPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

import { Operator } from "opendal";


const operator = new Operator("ftp", {

});


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import { Operator } from "opendal";


const operator = new Operator("ftp", {

  // endpoint of this backend

  // endpoint: "...",


  // root of this backend

  // root: "...",


  // user of this backend

  // user: "...",


  // password of this backend

  // password: "...",

});


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General4

| Key      | Type   | Required | Description              |
| -------- | ------ | -------- | ------------------------ |
| endpoint | string | no       | endpoint of this backend |
| root     | string | no       | root of this backend     |
| user     | string | no       | user of this backend     |
| password | string | no       | password of this backend |

# ftp

4 configuration options · available in Rust, Python, Node.js, Go

RustPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

import opendal


operator = opendal.Operator(

    "ftp",

)


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import opendal


operator = opendal.Operator(

    "ftp",

    # endpoint of this backend

    # endpoint="...",


    # root of this backend

    # root="...",


    # user of this backend

    # user="...",


    # password of this backend

    # password="...",

)


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General4

| Key      | Type   | Required | Description              |
| -------- | ------ | -------- | ------------------------ |
| endpoint | string | no       | endpoint of this backend |
| root     | string | no       | root of this backend     |
| user     | string | no       | user of this backend     |
| password | string | no       | password of this backend |

# gcs

14 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsRubyGo

Quick start

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/gcs"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(gcs.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	"bucket": "my-bucket",

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/gcs"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(gcs.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// --- General ---

	// root URI, all operations happens under `root`

	// "root": "/",


	// bucket name

	"bucket": "my-bucket",


	// endpoint URI of GCS service,

	// default is `https://storage.googleapis.com`

	// "endpoint": "https://storage.googleapis.com",


	// Scope for gcs.

	// "scope": "...",

	// --- Credentials ---

	// Service Account for gcs.

	// "service_account": "...",


	// Credentials string for GCS service OAuth2 authentication.

	// "credential": "...",


	// Local path to credentials file for GCS service OAuth2 authentication.

	// "credential_path": "...",


	// Skip signature will skip loading credentials and signing requests.

	// "skip_signature": "true",


	// Disable attempting to load credentials from the GCE metadata server when

	// running within Google Cloud.

	// "disable_vm_metadata": "true",


	// Disable loading configuration from the environment.

	// "disable_config_load": "true",


	// A Google Cloud OAuth2 token.

	//

	// Takes precedence over `credential` and `credential_path`.

	// "token": "...",

	// --- Behavior ---

	// The predefined acl for GCS.

	// "predefined_acl": "...",


	// The default storage class used by gcs.

	// "default_storage_class": "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General4

| Key                                            | Type   | Required | Description                                                            |
| ---------------------------------------------- | ------ | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| rootdefault /                                  | string | no       | root URI, all operations happens under root                            |
| buckete.g. my-bucket                           | string | yes      | bucket name                                                            |
| endpointdefault https://storage.googleapis.com | string | no       | endpoint URI of GCS service, default is https://storage.googleapis.com |
| scope                                          | string | no       | Scope for gcs.                                                         |

Credentials7

| Key                   | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                           |
| --------------------- | ------ | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| service\_account      | string | no       | Service Account for gcs.                                                                              |
| credential            | string | no       | Credentials string for GCS service OAuth2 authentication.                                             |
| credential\_path      | string | no       | Local path to credentials file for GCS service OAuth2 authentication.                                 |
| skip\_signature       | bool   | no       | Skip signature will skip loading credentials and signing requests.                                    |
| disable\_vm\_metadata | bool   | no       | Disable attempting to load credentials from the GCE metadata server when running within Google Cloud. |
| disable\_config\_load | bool   | no       | Disable loading configuration from the environment.                                                   |
| token                 | string | no       | A Google Cloud OAuth2 token. Takes precedence over credential and credential\_path.                   |

Behavior2

| Key                     | Type   | Required | Description                            |
| ----------------------- | ------ | -------- | -------------------------------------- |
| predefined\_acl         | string | no       | The predefined acl for GCS.            |
| default\_storage\_class | string | no       | The default storage class used by gcs. |

Deprecated1

| Key                        | Type | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                    |
| -------------------------- | ---- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| allow\_anonymousdeprecated | bool | no       | Allow opendal to send requests without signing when credentials are not loaded.Deprecated since 0.57.0: Please use skip\_signature instead of allow\_anonymous |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

Copyright © 2022-2026, The Apache Software Foundation  
Apache OpenDAL, OpenDAL, Apache, the Apache feather and the Apache OpenDAL project logo are either registered trademarks or trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.

---

---
url: https://opendal.apache.org/services/gcs/java/

# gcs

14 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsRubyGo

Quick start

```

import java.util.HashMap;

import java.util.Map;

import org.apache.opendal.Operator;


Map<String, String> config = new HashMap<>();

config.put("bucket", "my-bucket");

Operator operator = Operator.of("gcs", config);


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import java.util.HashMap;

import java.util.Map;

import org.apache.opendal.Operator;


Map<String, String> config = new HashMap<>();

// --- General ---

// root URI, all operations happens under `root`

// config.put("root", "/");


// bucket name

config.put("bucket", "my-bucket");


// endpoint URI of GCS service,

// default is `https://storage.googleapis.com`

// config.put("endpoint", "https://storage.googleapis.com");


// Scope for gcs.

// config.put("scope", "...");

// --- Credentials ---

// Service Account for gcs.

// config.put("service_account", "...");


// Credentials string for GCS service OAuth2 authentication.

// config.put("credential", "...");


// Local path to credentials file for GCS service OAuth2 authentication.

// config.put("credential_path", "...");


// Skip signature will skip loading credentials and signing requests.

// config.put("skip_signature", "true");


// Disable attempting to load credentials from the GCE metadata server when

// running within Google Cloud.

// config.put("disable_vm_metadata", "true");


// Disable loading configuration from the environment.

// config.put("disable_config_load", "true");


// A Google Cloud OAuth2 token.

//

// Takes precedence over `credential` and `credential_path`.

// config.put("token", "...");

// --- Behavior ---

// The predefined acl for GCS.

// config.put("predefined_acl", "...");


// The default storage class used by gcs.

// config.put("default_storage_class", "...");

Operator operator = Operator.of("gcs", config);


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General4

| Key                                            | Type   | Required | Description                                                            |
| ---------------------------------------------- | ------ | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| rootdefault /                                  | string | no       | root URI, all operations happens under root                            |
| buckete.g. my-bucket                           | string | yes      | bucket name                                                            |
| endpointdefault https://storage.googleapis.com | string | no       | endpoint URI of GCS service, default is https://storage.googleapis.com |
| scope                                          | string | no       | Scope for gcs.                                                         |

Credentials7

| Key                   | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                           |
| --------------------- | ------ | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| service\_account      | string | no       | Service Account for gcs.                                                                              |
| credential            | string | no       | Credentials string for GCS service OAuth2 authentication.                                             |
| credential\_path      | string | no       | Local path to credentials file for GCS service OAuth2 authentication.                                 |
| skip\_signature       | bool   | no       | Skip signature will skip loading credentials and signing requests.                                    |
| disable\_vm\_metadata | bool   | no       | Disable attempting to load credentials from the GCE metadata server when running within Google Cloud. |
| disable\_config\_load | bool   | no       | Disable loading configuration from the environment.                                                   |
| token                 | string | no       | A Google Cloud OAuth2 token. Takes precedence over credential and credential\_path.                   |

Behavior2

| Key                     | Type   | Required | Description                            |
| ----------------------- | ------ | -------- | -------------------------------------- |
| predefined\_acl         | string | no       | The predefined acl for GCS.            |
| default\_storage\_class | string | no       | The default storage class used by gcs. |

Deprecated1

| Key                        | Type | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                    |
| -------------------------- | ---- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| allow\_anonymousdeprecated | bool | no       | Allow opendal to send requests without signing when credentials are not loaded.Deprecated since 0.57.0: Please use skip\_signature instead of allow\_anonymous |

# gcs

14 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsRubyGo

Quick start

```

import { Operator } from "opendal";


const operator = new Operator("gcs", {

  bucket: "my-bucket",

});


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import { Operator } from "opendal";


const operator = new Operator("gcs", {

  // --- General ---

  // root URI, all operations happens under `root`

  // root: "/",


  // bucket name

  bucket: "my-bucket",


  // endpoint URI of GCS service,

  // default is `https://storage.googleapis.com`

  // endpoint: "https://storage.googleapis.com",


  // Scope for gcs.

  // scope: "...",

  // --- Credentials ---

  // Service Account for gcs.

  // service_account: "...",


  // Credentials string for GCS service OAuth2 authentication.

  // credential: "...",


  // Local path to credentials file for GCS service OAuth2 authentication.

  // credential_path: "...",


  // Skip signature will skip loading credentials and signing requests.

  // skip_signature: "true",


  // Disable attempting to load credentials from the GCE metadata server when

  // running within Google Cloud.

  // disable_vm_metadata: "true",


  // Disable loading configuration from the environment.

  // disable_config_load: "true",


  // A Google Cloud OAuth2 token.

  //

  // Takes precedence over `credential` and `credential_path`.

  // token: "...",

  // --- Behavior ---

  // The predefined acl for GCS.

  // predefined_acl: "...",


  // The default storage class used by gcs.

  // default_storage_class: "...",

});


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General4

| Key                                            | Type   | Required | Description                                                            |
| ---------------------------------------------- | ------ | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| rootdefault /                                  | string | no       | root URI, all operations happens under root                            |
| buckete.g. my-bucket                           | string | yes      | bucket name                                                            |
| endpointdefault https://storage.googleapis.com | string | no       | endpoint URI of GCS service, default is https://storage.googleapis.com |
| scope                                          | string | no       | Scope for gcs.                                                         |

| Key                   | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                           |
| --------------------- | ------ | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| service\_account      | string | no       | Service Account for gcs.                                                                              |
| credential            | string | no       | Credentials string for GCS service OAuth2 authentication.                                             |
| credential\_path      | string | no       | Local path to credentials file for GCS service OAuth2 authentication.                                 |
| skip\_signature       | bool   | no       | Skip signature will skip loading credentials and signing requests.                                    |
| disable\_vm\_metadata | bool   | no       | Disable attempting to load credentials from the GCE metadata server when running within Google Cloud. |
| disable\_config\_load | bool   | no       | Disable loading configuration from the environment.                                                   |
| token                 | string | no       | A Google Cloud OAuth2 token. Takes precedence over credential and credential\_path.                   |

    "gcs",

    # --- General ---

    # root URI, all operations happens under `root`

    # root="/",


    # bucket name

    bucket="my-bucket",


    # endpoint URI of GCS service,

    # default is `https://storage.googleapis.com`

    # endpoint="https://storage.googleapis.com",


    # Scope for gcs.

    # scope="...",

    # --- Credentials ---

    # Service Account for gcs.

    # service_account="...",


    # Credentials string for GCS service OAuth2 authentication.

    # credential="...",


    # Local path to credentials file for GCS service OAuth2 authentication.

    # credential_path="...",


    # Skip signature will skip loading credentials and signing requests.

    # skip_signature="true",


    # Disable attempting to load credentials from the GCE metadata server when

    # running within Google Cloud.

    # disable_vm_metadata="true",


    # Disable loading configuration from the environment.

    # disable_config_load="true",


    # A Google Cloud OAuth2 token.

    #

    # Takes precedence over `credential` and `credential_path`.

    # token="...",

    # --- Behavior ---

    # The predefined acl for GCS.

    # predefined_acl="...",


    # The default storage class used by gcs.

    # default_storage_class="...",

)

# gcs

14 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsRubyGo

Quick start

```

require "opendal"


operator = OpenDal::Operator.new("gcs", {

  "bucket" => "my-bucket",

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

require "opendal"


operator = OpenDal::Operator.new("gcs", {

  # --- General ---

  # root URI, all operations happens under `root`

  # "root" => "/",


  # bucket name

  "bucket" => "my-bucket",


  # endpoint URI of GCS service,

  # default is `https://storage.googleapis.com`

  # "endpoint" => "https://storage.googleapis.com",


  # Scope for gcs.

  # "scope" => "...",

  # --- Credentials ---

  # Service Account for gcs.

  # "service_account" => "...",


  # Credentials string for GCS service OAuth2 authentication.

  # "credential" => "...",


  # Local path to credentials file for GCS service OAuth2 authentication.

  # "credential_path" => "...",


  # Skip signature will skip loading credentials and signing requests.

  # "skip_signature" => "true",


  # Disable attempting to load credentials from the GCE metadata server when

  # running within Google Cloud.

  # "disable_vm_metadata" => "true",


  # Disable loading configuration from the environment.

  # "disable_config_load" => "true",


  # A Google Cloud OAuth2 token.

  #

  # Takes precedence over `credential` and `credential_path`.

  # "token" => "...",

  # --- Behavior ---

  # The predefined acl for GCS.

  # "predefined_acl" => "...",


  # The default storage class used by gcs.

  # "default_storage_class" => "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

| buckete.g. my-bucket                           | string | yes      | bucket name                                                            |
| endpointdefault https://storage.googleapis.com | string | no       | endpoint URI of GCS service, default is https://storage.googleapis.com |
| scope                                          | string | no       | Scope for gcs.                                                         |

| Key                   | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                           |
| --------------------- | ------ | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| service\_account      | string | no       | Service Account for gcs.                                                                              |
| credential            | string | no       | Credentials string for GCS service OAuth2 authentication.                                             |
| credential\_path      | string | no       | Local path to credentials file for GCS service OAuth2 authentication.                                 |
| skip\_signature       | bool   | no       | Skip signature will skip loading credentials and signing requests.                                    |
| disable\_vm\_metadata | bool   | no       | Disable attempting to load credentials from the GCE metadata server when running within Google Cloud. |
| disable\_config\_load | bool   | no       | Disable loading configuration from the environment.                                                   |

# gcs

14 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go

Quick start

    ("bucket".to_string(), "my-bucket".to_string()),

])?;


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

use opendal::Operator;


let operator = Operator::via_iter("gcs", [

    // --- General ---

    // root URI, all operations happens under `root`

    // ("root".to_string(), "/".to_string()),


    // bucket name

    ("bucket".to_string(), "my-bucket".to_string()),


    // endpoint URI of GCS service,

    // default is `https://storage.googleapis.com`

    // ("endpoint".to_string(), "https://storage.googleapis.com".to_string()),


    // Scope for gcs.

    // ("scope".to_string(), "...".to_string()),

    // --- Credentials ---

    // Service Account for gcs.

    // ("service_account".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // Credentials string for GCS service OAuth2 authentication.

    // ("credential".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // Local path to credentials file for GCS service OAuth2 authentication.

    // ("credential_path".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // Skip signature will skip loading credentials and signing requests.

    // ("skip_signature".to_string(), "true".to_string()),


    // Disable attempting to load credentials from the GCE metadata server when

    // running within Google Cloud.

    // ("disable_vm_metadata".to_string(), "true".to_string()),


    // Disable loading configuration from the environment.

    // ("disable_config_load".to_string(), "true".to_string()),


    // A Google Cloud OAuth2 token.

    //

    // Takes precedence over `credential` and `credential_path`.

    // ("token".to_string(), "...".to_string()),

    // --- Behavior ---

    // The predefined acl for GCS.

    // ("predefined_acl".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // The default storage class used by gcs.

    // ("default_storage_class".to_string(), "...".to_string()),

])?;


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/gdrive"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)

	// The root for gdrive

	// "root": "...",


	// Access token for gdrive.

	// "access_token": "...",


	// Refresh token for gdrive.

	// "refresh_token": "...",


	// Client id for gdrive.

	// "client_id": "...",


	// Client secret for gdrive.

	// "client_secret": "...",

})

})

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/ghac"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(ghac.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// The root path for ghac.

	// "root": "...",


	// The version that used by cache.

	// "version": "...",


	// The endpoint for ghac service.

	// "endpoint": "...",


	// The runtime token for ghac service.

	// "runtime_token": "...",

})


```

| -------------- | ------ | -------- | ----------------------------------- |

| version        | string | no       | The version that used by cache.     |
| endpoint       | string | no       | The endpoint for ghac service.      |
| runtime\_token | string | no       | The runtime token for ghac service. |

# github

4 configuration options · available in Rust, Python, Go

RustPythonGo

Quick start

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/github"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(github.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	"owner": "...",

	"repo": "...",

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/github"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(github.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// root of this backend.

	//

	// All operations will happen under this root.

	// "root": "...",


	// GitHub access_token.

	//

	// optional.

	// If not provided, the backend will only support read operations for public repositories.

	// And rate limit will be limited to 60 requests per hour.

	// "token": "...",


	// GitHub repo owner.

	//

	// required.

	"owner": "...",


	// GitHub repo name.

	//

	// required.

	"repo": "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General4

| Key   | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                     |
| ----- | ------ | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| root  | string | no       | root of this backend. All operations will happen under this root.                                                                                                               |
| token | string | no       | GitHub access\_token. optional. If not provided, the backend will only support read operations for public repositories. And rate limit will be limited to 60 requests per hour. |
| owner | string | yes      | GitHub repo owner. required.                                                                                                                                                    |
| repo  | string | yes      | GitHub repo name. required.                                                                                                                                                     |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

Copyright © 2022-2026, The Apache Software Foundation  
Apache OpenDAL, OpenDAL, Apache, the Apache feather and the Apache OpenDAL project logo are either registered trademarks or trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.

---

---
url: https://opendal.apache.org/services/github/python/
---

# github

4 configuration options · available in Rust, Python, Go

RustPythonGo

Quick start

```

import opendal


operator = opendal.Operator(

    "github",

    owner="...",

    repo="...",

)


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import opendal


operator = opendal.Operator(

    "github",

    # root of this backend.

    #

    # All operations will happen under this root.

    # root="...",


    # GitHub access_token.

    #

    # optional.

    # If not provided, the backend will only support read operations for public repositories.

    # And rate limit will be limited to 60 requests per hour.

    # token="...",


    # GitHub repo owner.

    #

    # required.

    owner="...",


    # GitHub repo name.

    #

    # required.

    repo="...",

)


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General4

| Key   | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                     |
| ----- | ------ | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| root  | string | no       | root of this backend. All operations will happen under this root.                                                                                                               |
| token | string | no       | GitHub access\_token. optional. If not provided, the backend will only support read operations for public repositories. And rate limit will be limited to 60 requests per hour. |
| owner | string | yes      | GitHub repo owner. required.                                                                                                                                                    |
| repo  | string | yes      | GitHub repo name. required.                                                                                                                                                     |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

Copyright © 2022-2026, The Apache Software Foundation  
Apache OpenDAL, OpenDAL, Apache, the Apache feather and the Apache OpenDAL project logo are either registered trademarks or trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.

url: https://opendal.apache.org/services/github/rust/

# github

4 configuration options · available in Rust, Python, Go

RustPythonGo

Quick start

```

use opendal::Operator;


let operator = Operator::via_iter("github", [

    ("owner".to_string(), "...".to_string()),

    ("repo".to_string(), "...".to_string()),

])?;


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

use opendal::Operator;


let operator = Operator::via_iter("github", [

    // root of this backend.

    //

    // All operations will happen under this root.

    // ("root".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // GitHub access_token.

    //

    // optional.

    // If not provided, the backend will only support read operations for public repositories.

    // And rate limit will be limited to 60 requests per hour.

    // ("token".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // GitHub repo owner.

    //

    // required.

    ("owner".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // GitHub repo name.

    //

    // required.

    ("repo".to_string(), "...".to_string()),

])?;


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General4

| Key   | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                     |
| ----- | ------ | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| root  | string | no       | root of this backend. All operations will happen under this root.                                                                                                               |
| token | string | no       | GitHub access\_token. optional. If not provided, the backend will only support read operations for public repositories. And rate limit will be limited to 60 requests per hour. |
| owner | string | yes      | GitHub repo owner. required.                                                                                                                                                    |
| repo  | string | yes      | GitHub repo name. required.                                                                                                                                                     |

# goosefs

7 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/goosefs"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(goosefs.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/goosefs"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(goosefs.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// Root path of this backend.

	//

	// All operations will happen under this root.

	// Default to `/` if not set.

	// "root": "...",


	// Master address(es) in `host:port` format.

	//

	// For single master: `"10.0.0.1:9200"`

	// For HA (comma-separated): `"10.0.0.1:9200,10.0.0.2:9200,10.0.0.3:9200"`

	//

	// When multiple addresses are provided, the client uses

	// `PollingMasterInquireClient` to discover the Primary Master automatically.

	//

	// Resolution precedence at `build()` time (highest → lowest), following

	// `goosefs-sdk` `docs/CLIENT_CONFIGURATION.md` §1:

	// 1. This field (when set on the builder / OpenDAL config map)

	// 2. `GOOSEFS_MASTER_ADDR` environment variable

	// 3. `goosefs.master.rpc.addresses` / `goosefs.master.hostname` in

	// `goosefs-site.properties`

	//

	// `build()` fails with `ConfigInvalid` only when **none** of the above

	// supplies a master address.

	// "master_addr": "...",


	// Block size in bytes for new files (default: 64 MiB).

	// "block_size": "1000",


	// Chunk size in bytes for streaming RPCs (default: 1 MiB).

	// "chunk_size": "1000",


	// Default write type for new files.

	//

	// Supported values: `"must_cache"`, `"cache_through"`, `"through"`, `"async_through"`.

	// Default: `"must_cache"`.

	// "write_type": "...",


	// Authentication type.

	//

	// Supported values: `"nosasl"`, `"simple"`.

	// Default: `"simple"` — PLAIN SASL with username.

	// `"nosasl"` — skip authentication entirely.

	// "auth_type": "...",


	// Authentication username.

	//

	// Used in SIMPLE mode as the login identity.

	// Default: current OS user (`$USER` / `$USERNAME`).

	// "auth_username": "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General7

| Key            | Type    | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| -------------- | ------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| root           | string  | no       | Root path of this backend. All operations will happen under this root. Default to / if not set.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| master\_addr   | string  | no       | Master address(es) in host:port format. For single master: "10.0.0.1:9200"For HA (comma-separated): "10.0.0.1:9200,10.0.0.2:9200,10.0.0.3:9200"When multiple addresses are provided, the client usesPollingMasterInquireClient to discover the Primary Master automatically. Resolution precedence at build() time (highest → lowest), followinggoosefs-sdk docs/CLIENT\_CONFIGURATION.md §1: 1\. This field (when set on the builder / OpenDAL config map) 2\. GOOSEFS\_MASTER\_ADDR environment variable 3\. goosefs.master.rpc.addresses / goosefs.master.hostname ingoosefs-site.properties build() fails with ConfigInvalid only when \*\*none\*\* of the above supplies a master address. |
| block\_size    | integer | no       | Block size in bytes for new files (default: 64 MiB).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| chunk\_size    | integer | no       | Chunk size in bytes for streaming RPCs (default: 1 MiB).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| write\_type    | string  | no       | Default write type for new files. Supported values: "must\_cache", "cache\_through", "through", "async\_through". Default: "must\_cache".                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| auth\_type     | string  | no       | Authentication type. Supported values: "nosasl", "simple". Default: "simple" — PLAIN SASL with username."nosasl" — skip authentication entirely.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| auth\_username | string  | no       | Authentication username. Used in SIMPLE mode as the login identity. Default: current OS user ($USER / $USERNAME).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

Apache OpenDAL, OpenDAL, Apache, the Apache feather and the Apache OpenDAL project logo are either registered trademarks or trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.

# goosefs

7 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

import java.util.HashMap;

import java.util.Map;

import org.apache.opendal.Operator;


Map<String, String> config = new HashMap<>();

Operator operator = Operator.of("goosefs", config);


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import java.util.HashMap;

import java.util.Map;

import org.apache.opendal.Operator;


Map<String, String> config = new HashMap<>();

// Root path of this backend.

//

// All operations will happen under this root.

// Default to `/` if not set.

// config.put("root", "...");


// Master address(es) in `host:port` format.

//

// For single master: `"10.0.0.1:9200"`

// For HA (comma-separated): `"10.0.0.1:9200,10.0.0.2:9200,10.0.0.3:9200"`

//

// When multiple addresses are provided, the client uses

// `PollingMasterInquireClient` to discover the Primary Master automatically.

//

// Resolution precedence at `build()` time (highest → lowest), following

// `goosefs-sdk` `docs/CLIENT_CONFIGURATION.md` §1:

// 1. This field (when set on the builder / OpenDAL config map)

// 2. `GOOSEFS_MASTER_ADDR` environment variable

// 3. `goosefs.master.rpc.addresses` / `goosefs.master.hostname` in

// `goosefs-site.properties`

//

// `build()` fails with `ConfigInvalid` only when **none** of the above

// supplies a master address.

// config.put("master_addr", "...");


// Block size in bytes for new files (default: 64 MiB).

// config.put("block_size", "1000");


// Chunk size in bytes for streaming RPCs (default: 1 MiB).

// config.put("chunk_size", "1000");


// Default write type for new files.

//

// Supported values: `"must_cache"`, `"cache_through"`, `"through"`, `"async_through"`.

// Default: `"must_cache"`.

// config.put("write_type", "...");


// Authentication type.

//

// Supported values: `"nosasl"`, `"simple"`.

// Default: `"simple"` — PLAIN SASL with username.

// `"nosasl"` — skip authentication entirely.

// config.put("auth_type", "...");


// Authentication username.

//

// Used in SIMPLE mode as the login identity.

// Default: current OS user (`$USER` / `$USERNAME`).

// config.put("auth_username", "...");

Operator operator = Operator.of("goosefs", config);


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General7

| Key            | Type    | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| -------------- | ------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| root           | string  | no       | Root path of this backend. All operations will happen under this root. Default to / if not set.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| master\_addr   | string  | no       | Master address(es) in host:port format. For single master: "10.0.0.1:9200"For HA (comma-separated): "10.0.0.1:9200,10.0.0.2:9200,10.0.0.3:9200"When multiple addresses are provided, the client usesPollingMasterInquireClient to discover the Primary Master automatically. GOOSEFS\_MASTER\_ADDR environment variable 3\. goosefs.master.rpc.addresses / goosefs.master.hostname ingoosefs-site.properties build() fails with ConfigInvalid only when \*\*none\*\* of the above supplies a master address. |
| block\_size    | integer | no       | Block size in bytes for new files (default: 64 MiB).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| chunk\_size    | integer | no       | Chunk size in bytes for streaming RPCs (default: 1 MiB).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |

# goosefs

7 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

import { Operator } from "opendal";


const operator = new Operator("goosefs", {

});


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import { Operator } from "opendal";


const operator = new Operator("goosefs", {

  // Root path of this backend.

  //

  // All operations will happen under this root.

  // Default to `/` if not set.

  // root: "...",


  // Master address(es) in `host:port` format.

  //

  // For single master: `"10.0.0.1:9200"`

  // For HA (comma-separated): `"10.0.0.1:9200,10.0.0.2:9200,10.0.0.3:9200"`

  //

  // When multiple addresses are provided, the client uses

  // `PollingMasterInquireClient` to discover the Primary Master automatically.

  //

  // Resolution precedence at `build()` time (highest → lowest), following

  // `goosefs-sdk` `docs/CLIENT_CONFIGURATION.md` §1:

  // 1. This field (when set on the builder / OpenDAL config map)

  // 2. `GOOSEFS_MASTER_ADDR` environment variable

  // 3. `goosefs.master.rpc.addresses` / `goosefs.master.hostname` in

  // `goosefs-site.properties`

  //

  // `build()` fails with `ConfigInvalid` only when **none** of the above

  // supplies a master address.

  // master_addr: "...",


  // Block size in bytes for new files (default: 64 MiB).

  // block_size: "1000",


  // Chunk size in bytes for streaming RPCs (default: 1 MiB).

  // chunk_size: "1000",


  // Default write type for new files.

  //

  // Supported values: `"must_cache"`, `"cache_through"`, `"through"`, `"async_through"`.

  // Default: `"must_cache"`.

  // write_type: "...",


  // Authentication type.

  //

  // Supported values: `"nosasl"`, `"simple"`.

  // Default: `"simple"` — PLAIN SASL with username.

  // `"nosasl"` — skip authentication entirely.

  // auth_type: "...",


  // Authentication username.

  //

  // Used in SIMPLE mode as the login identity.

  // Default: current OS user (`$USER` / `$USERNAME`).

  // auth_username: "...",

});


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

import opendal

    "goosefs",

    #

    # All operations will happen under this root.

    # Default to `/` if not set.

    # root="...",


    # Master address(es) in `host:port` format.

    #

    # For single master: `"10.0.0.1:9200"`

    # For HA (comma-separated): `"10.0.0.1:9200,10.0.0.2:9200,10.0.0.3:9200"`

    #

    # When multiple addresses are provided, the client uses

    # `PollingMasterInquireClient` to discover the Primary Master automatically.

    #

    # Resolution precedence at `build()` time (highest → lowest), following

    # `goosefs-sdk` `docs/CLIENT_CONFIGURATION.md` §1:

    # 1. This field (when set on the builder / OpenDAL config map)

    # 2. `GOOSEFS_MASTER_ADDR` environment variable

    # 3. `goosefs.master.rpc.addresses` / `goosefs.master.hostname` in

    # `goosefs-site.properties`

    #

    # `build()` fails with `ConfigInvalid` only when **none** of the above

    # supplies a master address.

    # master_addr="...",


    # Block size in bytes for new files (default: 64 MiB).

    # block_size="1000",


    # Chunk size in bytes for streaming RPCs (default: 1 MiB).

    # chunk_size="1000",


    # Default write type for new files.

    #

    # Supported values: `"must_cache"`, `"cache_through"`, `"through"`, `"async_through"`.

    # Default: `"must_cache"`.

    # write_type="...",


    # Authentication type.

    #

    # Supported values: `"nosasl"`, `"simple"`.

    # Default: `"simple"` — PLAIN SASL with username.

    # `"nosasl"` — skip authentication entirely.

    # auth_type="...",


    # Authentication username.

    #

    # Used in SIMPLE mode as the login identity.

    # Default: current OS user (`$USER` / `$USERNAME`).

    # auth_username="...",

)


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General7

| Key            | Type    | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| -------------- | ------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| root           | string  | no       | Root path of this backend. All operations will happen under this root. Default to / if not set.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| master\_addr   | string  | no       | Master address(es) in host:port format. For single master: "10.0.0.1:9200"For HA (comma-separated): "10.0.0.1:9200,10.0.0.2:9200,10.0.0.3:9200"When multiple addresses are provided, the client usesPollingMasterInquireClient to discover the Primary Master automatically. Resolution precedence at build() time (highest → lowest), followinggoosefs-sdk docs/CLIENT\_CONFIGURATION.md §1: 1\. This field (when set on the builder / OpenDAL config map) 2\. GOOSEFS\_MASTER\_ADDR environment variable 3\. goosefs.master.rpc.addresses / goosefs.master.hostname ingoosefs-site.properties build() fails with ConfigInvalid only when \*\*none\*\* of the above supplies a master address. |
| block\_size    | integer | no       | Block size in bytes for new files (default: 64 MiB).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| chunk\_size    | integer | no       | Chunk size in bytes for streaming RPCs (default: 1 MiB).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| write\_type    | string  | no       | Default write type for new files. Supported values: "must\_cache", "cache\_through", "through", "async\_through". Default: "must\_cache".                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| auth\_type     | string  | no       | Authentication type. Supported values: "nosasl", "simple". Default: "simple" — PLAIN SASL with username."nosasl" — skip authentication entirely.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| auth\_username | string  | no       | Authentication username. Used in SIMPLE mode as the login identity. Default: current OS user ($USER / $USERNAME).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |

# goosefs

])?;

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

use opendal::Operator;


let operator = Operator::via_iter("goosefs", [

    // Root path of this backend.

    //

    // All operations will happen under this root.

    // Default to `/` if not set.

    // ("root".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // Master address(es) in `host:port` format.

    //

    // For single master: `"10.0.0.1:9200"`

    // For HA (comma-separated): `"10.0.0.1:9200,10.0.0.2:9200,10.0.0.3:9200"`

    //

    // When multiple addresses are provided, the client uses

    // `PollingMasterInquireClient` to discover the Primary Master automatically.

    //

    // Resolution precedence at `build()` time (highest → lowest), following

    // `goosefs-sdk` `docs/CLIENT_CONFIGURATION.md` §1:

    // 1. This field (when set on the builder / OpenDAL config map)

    // 2. `GOOSEFS_MASTER_ADDR` environment variable

    // 3. `goosefs.master.rpc.addresses` / `goosefs.master.hostname` in

    // `goosefs-site.properties`

    //

    // `build()` fails with `ConfigInvalid` only when **none** of the above

    // supplies a master address.

    // ("master_addr".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // Block size in bytes for new files (default: 64 MiB).

    // ("block_size".to_string(), "1000".to_string()),


    // Chunk size in bytes for streaming RPCs (default: 1 MiB).

    // ("chunk_size".to_string(), "1000".to_string()),


    // Default write type for new files.

    //

    // Supported values: `"must_cache"`, `"cache_through"`, `"through"`, `"async_through"`.

    // Default: `"must_cache"`.

    // ("write_type".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // Authentication type.

    //

    // Supported values: `"nosasl"`, `"simple"`.

    // Default: `"simple"` — PLAIN SASL with username.

    // `"nosasl"` — skip authentication entirely.

    // ("auth_type".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // Authentication username.

    //

    // Used in SIMPLE mode as the login identity.

    // Default: current OS user (`$USER` / `$USERNAME`).

    // ("auth_username".to_string(), "...".to_string()),

])?;


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

# gridfs

5 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/gridfs"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(gridfs.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/gridfs"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(gridfs.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// The connection string of the MongoDB service.

	// "connection_string": "...",


	// The database name of the MongoDB GridFs service to read/write.

	// "database": "...",


	// The bucket name of the MongoDB GridFs service to read/write.

	// "bucket": "...",


	// The chunk size of the MongoDB GridFs service used to break the user file into chunks.

	// "chunk_size": "1000",


	// The working directory, all operations will be performed under it.

	// "root": "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General5

| Key                | Type    | Required | Description                                                                           |
| ------------------ | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| connection\_string | string  | no       | The connection string of the MongoDB service.                                         |
| database           | string  | no       | The database name of the MongoDB GridFs service to read/write.                        |
| bucket             | string  | no       | The bucket name of the MongoDB GridFs service to read/write.                          |
| chunk\_size        | integer | no       | The chunk size of the MongoDB GridFs service used to break the user file into chunks. |
| root               | string  | no       | The working directory, all operations will be performed under it.                     |

| bucket             | string  | no       | The bucket name of the MongoDB GridFs service to read/write.                          |
| chunk\_size        | integer | no       | The chunk size of the MongoDB GridFs service used to break the user file into chunks. |
| root               | string  | no       | The working directory, all operations will be performed under it.                     |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

Copyright © 2022-2026, The Apache Software Foundation  
Apache OpenDAL, OpenDAL, Apache, the Apache feather and the Apache OpenDAL project logo are either registered trademarks or trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.

---

---
url: https://opendal.apache.org/services/hdfs-native/go/

4 configuration options · available in Rust, Python, Node.js, Go

RustPythonNode.jsGo

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/hdfs-native"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/hdfs-native"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(hdfs-native.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// work dir of this backend

	// "root": "...",


	// name_node of this backend

	// "name_node": "...",


	// other options for hdfs client

	// "options": "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General4

| Key                      | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                       |
| ------------------------ | ------ | -------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| root                     | string | no       | work dir of this backend                                                                                                                                                          |
| name\_node               | string | no       | name\_node of this backend                                                                                                                                                        |
| enable\_appenddeprecated | bool   | no       | Deprecated: HDFS Native append capability is enabled by default.Deprecated since 0.57.0: HDFS Native append capability is enabled by default and this option is no longer needed. |
| options                  | map    | no       | other options for hdfs client                                                                                                                                                     |

# hdfs

6 configuration options · available in Rust, Python, Node.js, Go

RustPythonNode.jsGo

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/hdfs"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/hdfs"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(hdfs.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// work dir of this backend

	// "root": "...",


	// name node of this backend

	// "name_node": "...",


	// kerberos_ticket_cache_path of this backend

	// "kerberos_ticket_cache_path": "...",


	// user of this backend

	// "user": "...",


	// atomic_write_dir of this backend

	// "atomic_write_dir": "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General6

| Key                           | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                         |
| ----------------------------- | ------ | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| root                          | string | no       | work dir of this backend                                                                                                                                            |
| name\_node                    | string | no       | name node of this backend                                                                                                                                           |
| kerberos\_ticket\_cache\_path | string | no       | kerberos\_ticket\_cache\_path of this backend                                                                                                                       |
| user                          | string | no       | user of this backend                                                                                                                                                |
| enable\_appenddeprecated      | bool   | no       | Deprecated: HDFS append capability is enabled by default.Deprecated since 0.57.0: HDFS append capability is enabled by default and this option is no longer needed. |
| atomic\_write\_dir            | string | no       | atomic\_write\_dir of this backend                                                                                                                                  |
