# hf

7 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/hf"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(hf.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/hf"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(hf.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// Repo type of this backend. Required.

	// "repo_type": "...",


	// Repo id of this backend.

	//

	// This is required.

	// "repo_id": "...",


	// Revision of this backend.

	//

	// Default is main.

	// "revision": "...",


	// Root of this backend. Can be "/path/to/dir".

	//

	// Default is "/".

	// "root": "...",


	// Token of this backend.

	//

	// This is optional.

	// "token": "...",


	// Endpoint of the Hugging Face Hub.

	//

	// Default is "https://huggingface.co".

	// "endpoint": "...",


	// Download mode. Either `xet` (default) or `http`.

	//

	// When unset, the mode is resolved from the `HF_HUB_DISABLE_XET`

	// environment variable: a non-empty value forces `http`, otherwise it

	// defaults to `xet`. An explicit value here takes precedence.

	//

	// See <https://huggingface.co/docs/huggingface_hub/package_reference/environment_variables#hfhubdisablexet>.

	// "download_mode": "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General7

| Key            | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| -------------- | ------ | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| repo\_type     | string | no       | Repo type of this backend. Required.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| repo\_id       | string | no       | Repo id of this backend. This is required.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| revision       | string | no       | Revision of this backend. Default is main.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| root           | string | no       | Root of this backend. Can be "/path/to/dir". Default is "/".                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| token          | string | no       | Token of this backend. This is optional.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| endpoint       | string | no       | Endpoint of the Hugging Face Hub. Default is "https://huggingface.co".                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| download\_mode | string | no       | Download mode. Either xet (default) or http. When unset, the mode is resolved from the HF\_HUB\_DISABLE\_XETenvironment variable: a non-empty value forces http, otherwise it defaults to xet. An explicit value here takes precedence. See <https://huggingface.co/docs/huggingface\_hub/package\_reference/environment\_variables#hfhubdisablexet>. |

```
  // Repo type of this backend. Required.

  // repo_type: "...",


  // Repo id of this backend.

  //

  // This is required.

  // repo_id: "...",


  // Revision of this backend.

  //

  // Default is main.

  // revision: "...",


  // Root of this backend. Can be "/path/to/dir".

  //

  // Default is "/".

  // root: "...",


  // Token of this backend.

  //

  // This is optional.

  // token: "...",

  //

  // Default is "https://huggingface.co".

  // endpoint: "...",


  // Download mode. Either `xet` (default) or `http`.

  //

  // When unset, the mode is resolved from the `HF_HUB_DISABLE_XET`

  // environment variable: a non-empty value forces `http`, otherwise it

  // defaults to `xet`. An explicit value here takes precedence.

  //

  // See <https://huggingface.co/docs/huggingface_hub/package_reference/environment_variables#hfhubdisablexet>.

  // download_mode: "...",

});
```

| -------- | ------ | -------- | ------------------------ |
| endpoint | string | no       | endpoint of this backend |
| username | string | no       | username of this backend |
| password | string | no       | password of this backend |
| token    | string | no       | token of this backend    |
| root     | string | no       | root of this backend     |

# http

RustJavaPythonNode.jsRubyGo

Quick start

```

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

require "opendal"


operator = OpenDal::Operator.new("http", {

  # endpoint of this backend

  # "endpoint" => "...",


  # username of this backend

  # "username" => "...",


  # password of this backend

  # "password" => "...",


  # token of this backend

  # "token" => "...",


  # root of this backend

  # "root" => "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

# http

5 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsRubyGo

Quick start

```

use opendal::Operator;


let operator = Operator::via_iter("http", [

])?;


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

use opendal::Operator;


let operator = Operator::via_iter("http", [

    // endpoint of this backend

    // ("endpoint".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // username of this backend

    // ("username".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // password of this backend

    // ("password".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // token of this backend

    // ("token".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // root of this backend

    // ("root".to_string(), "...".to_string()),

])?;


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General5

| Key      | Type   | Required | Description              |
| -------- | ------ | -------- | ------------------------ |
| endpoint | string | no       | endpoint of this backend |
| username | string | no       | username of this backend |
| password | string | no       | password of this backend |
| token    | string | no       | token of this backend    |
| root     | string | no       | root of this backend     |

# ipfs

2 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/ipfs"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(ipfs.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/ipfs"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(ipfs.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// IPFS gateway endpoint.

	// "endpoint": "...",


	// IPFS root.

	// "root": "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General2

| Key      | Type   | Required | Description            |
| -------- | ------ | -------- | ---------------------- |
| endpoint | string | no       | IPFS gateway endpoint. |
| root     | string | no       | IPFS root.             |

# ipfs

RustJavaPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

import java.util.HashMap;

import java.util.Map;

import org.apache.opendal.Operator;

Operator operator = Operator.of("ipfs", config);


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import java.util.HashMap;

import java.util.Map;

import org.apache.opendal.Operator;


Map<String, String> config = new HashMap<>();

// IPFS gateway endpoint.

// config.put("endpoint", "...");


// IPFS root.

// config.put("root", "...");

Operator operator = Operator.of("ipfs", config);


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

# ipmfs

```
	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/ipmfs"

)

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/ipmfs"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(ipmfs.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// Root for ipfs.

	// "root": "...",


	// Endpoint for ipfs.

	// "endpoint": "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General2

| Key      | Type   | Required | Description        |
| -------- | ------ | -------- | ------------------ |
| root     | string | no       | Root for ipfs.     |
| endpoint | string | no       | Endpoint for ipfs. |

# ipmfs

2 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsRubyGo

Quick start

```

import java.util.HashMap;

import java.util.Map;

import org.apache.opendal.Operator;


Map<String, String> config = new HashMap<>();

Operator operator = Operator.of("ipmfs", config);


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import java.util.HashMap;

import java.util.Map;

import org.apache.opendal.Operator;


Map<String, String> config = new HashMap<>();

// Root for ipfs.

// config.put("root", "...");


// Endpoint for ipfs.

// config.put("endpoint", "...");

Operator operator = Operator.of("ipmfs", config);


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General2

| Key      | Type   | Required | Description        |
| -------- | ------ | -------- | ------------------ |
| root     | string | no       | Root for ipfs.     |
| endpoint | string | no       | Endpoint for ipfs. |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

Apache OpenDAL, OpenDAL, Apache, the Apache feather and the Apache OpenDAL project logo are either registered trademarks or trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.

# ipmfs

2 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsRubyGo

Quick start

```

import { Operator } from "opendal";


const operator = new Operator("ipmfs", {

});


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import { Operator } from "opendal";


const operator = new Operator("ipmfs", {

  // Root for ipfs.

  // root: "...",


  // Endpoint for ipfs.

  // endpoint: "...",

});


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General2

| Key      | Type   | Required | Description        |
| -------- | ------ | -------- | ------------------ |
| root     | string | no       | Root for ipfs.     |
| endpoint | string | no       | Endpoint for ipfs. |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

Copyright © 2022-2026, The Apache Software Foundation  
Apache OpenDAL, OpenDAL, Apache, the Apache feather and the Apache OpenDAL project logo are either registered trademarks or trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.

# ipmfs

2 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsRubyGo

Quick start

```

import opendal


operator = opendal.Operator(

    "ipmfs",

)


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import opendal


operator = opendal.Operator(

    "ipmfs",

    # Root for ipfs.

    # root="...",


    # Endpoint for ipfs.

    # endpoint="...",

)


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General2

| Key      | Type   | Required | Description        |
| -------- | ------ | -------- | ------------------ |
| root     | string | no       | Root for ipfs.     |
| endpoint | string | no       | Endpoint for ipfs. |

# ipmfs

2 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsRubyGo

Quick start

```

require "opendal"


operator = OpenDal::Operator.new("ipmfs", {

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

require "opendal"


operator = OpenDal::Operator.new("ipmfs", {

  # Root for ipfs.

  # "root" => "...",


  # Endpoint for ipfs.

  # "endpoint" => "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General2

| Key      | Type   | Required | Description        |
| -------- | ------ | -------- | ------------------ |
| root     | string | no       | Root for ipfs.     |
| endpoint | string | no       | Endpoint for ipfs. |

# ipmfs

2 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsRubyGo

Quick start

```

use opendal::Operator;


let operator = Operator::via_iter("ipmfs", [

])?;


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

use opendal::Operator;


let operator = Operator::via_iter("ipmfs", [

    // Root for ipfs.

    // ("root".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // Endpoint for ipfs.

    // ("endpoint".to_string(), "...".to_string()),

])?;


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General2

| Key      | Type   | Required | Description        |
| -------- | ------ | -------- | ------------------ |
| root     | string | no       | Root for ipfs.     |
| endpoint | string | no       | Endpoint for ipfs. |

# koofr

4 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/koofr"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(koofr.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	"endpoint": "...",

	"email": "...",

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/koofr"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(koofr.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// root of this backend.

	//

	// All operations will happen under this root.

	// "root": "...",


	// Koofr endpoint.

	"endpoint": "...",


	// Koofr email.

	"email": "...",


	// password of this backend. (Must be the application password)

	// "password": "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General4

| Key      | Type   | Required | Description                                                       |
| -------- | ------ | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| root     | string | no       | root of this backend. All operations will happen under this root. |
| endpoint | string | yes      | Koofr endpoint.                                                   |
| email    | string | yes      | Koofr email.                                                      |
| password | string | no       | password of this backend. (Must be the application password)      |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

Copyright © 2022-2026, The Apache Software Foundation  
Apache OpenDAL, OpenDAL, Apache, the Apache feather and the Apache OpenDAL project logo are either registered trademarks or trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.

---

---
url: https://opendal.apache.org/services/koofr/java/
---

# koofr

4 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

import java.util.HashMap;

import java.util.Map;

import org.apache.opendal.Operator;


Map<String, String> config = new HashMap<>();

config.put("endpoint", "...");

config.put("email", "...");

Operator operator = Operator.of("koofr", config);


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import java.util.HashMap;

import java.util.Map;

import org.apache.opendal.Operator;


Map<String, String> config = new HashMap<>();

// root of this backend.

//

// All operations will happen under this root.

// config.put("root", "...");


// Koofr endpoint.

config.put("endpoint", "...");


// Koofr email.

config.put("email", "...");


// password of this backend. (Must be the application password)

// config.put("password", "...");

Operator operator = Operator.of("koofr", config);


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General4

| Key      | Type   | Required | Description                                                       |
| -------- | ------ | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| root     | string | no       | root of this backend. All operations will happen under this root. |
| endpoint | string | yes      | Koofr endpoint.                                                   |
| email    | string | yes      | Koofr email.                                                      |
| password | string | no       | password of this backend. (Must be the application password)      |

4 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```
import { Operator } from "opendal";

  endpoint: "...",

  email: "...",
```

Full reference

```

import { Operator } from "opendal";


const operator = new Operator("koofr", {

  // root of this backend.

  //

  // All operations will happen under this root.

  // root: "...",

  endpoint: "...",


  // Koofr email.

  email: "...",


  // password of this backend. (Must be the application password)

  // password: "...",

});


```

# koofr

4 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

use opendal::Operator;


let operator = Operator::via_iter("koofr", [

    ("endpoint".to_string(), "...".to_string()),

    ("email".to_string(), "...".to_string()),

])?;


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

use opendal::Operator;


let operator = Operator::via_iter("koofr", [

    // root of this backend.

    //

    // All operations will happen under this root.

    // ("root".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // Koofr endpoint.

    ("endpoint".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // Koofr email.

    ("email".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // password of this backend. (Must be the application password)

    // ("password".to_string(), "...".to_string()),

])?;


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General4

| Key      | Type   | Required | Description                                                       |
| -------- | ------ | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| root     | string | no       | root of this backend. All operations will happen under this root. |
| endpoint | string | yes      | Koofr endpoint.                                                   |
| email    | string | yes      | Koofr email.                                                      |
| password | string | no       | password of this backend. (Must be the application password)      |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

Apache OpenDAL, OpenDAL, Apache, the Apache feather and the Apache OpenDAL project logo are either registered trademarks or trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/lakefs"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(lakefs.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// Base url.

	//

	// This is required.

	// "endpoint": "...",


	// Username for Lakefs basic authentication.

	//

	// This is required.

	// "username": "...",


	// Password for Lakefs basic authentication.

	//

	// This is required.

	// "password": "...",


	// Root of this backend. Can be "/path/to/dir".

	//

	// Default is "/".

	// "root": "...",


	// The repository name

	//

	// This is required.

	// "repository": "...",


	// Name of the branch or a commit ID. Default is main.

	//

	// This is optional.

	// "branch": "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General6

| username   | string | no       | Username for Lakefs basic authentication. This is required.           |
| password   | string | no       | Password for Lakefs basic authentication. This is required.           |
| root       | string | no       | Root of this backend. Can be "/path/to/dir". Default is "/".          |
| repository | string | no       | The repository name This is required.                                 |
| branch     | string | no       | Name of the branch or a commit ID. Default is main. This is optional. |

# memcached

6 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/memcached"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(memcached.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/memcached"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(memcached.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// network address of the memcached service.

	//

	// For example: "tcp://localhost:11211"

	// "endpoint": "...",


	// the working directory of the service. Can be "/path/to/dir"

	//

	// default is "/"

	// "root": "...",


	// Memcached username, optional.

	// "username": "...",


	// Memcached password, optional.

	// "password": "...",


	// The default ttl for put operations.

	// "default_ttl": "10s",


	// The maximum number of connections allowed.

	//

	// default is 10

	// "connection_pool_max_size": "1000",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General6

| Key                         | Type     | Required | Description                                                                    |
| --------------------------- | -------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| endpoint                    | string   | no       | network address of the memcached service. For example: "tcp://localhost:11211" |
| root                        | string   | no       | the working directory of the service. Can be "/path/to/dir" default is "/"     |
| username                    | string   | no       | Memcached username, optional.                                                  |
| password                    | string   | no       | Memcached password, optional.                                                  |
| default\_ttl                | duration | no       | The default ttl for put operations.                                            |
| connection\_pool\_max\_size | integer  | no       | The maximum number of connections allowed. default is 10                       |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```
// config.put("endpoint", "...");
```

| root                        | string   | no       | the working directory of the service. Can be "/path/to/dir" default is "/"     |
| username                    | string   | no       | Memcached username, optional.                                                  |

Full reference

```

use opendal::Operator;


let operator = Operator::via_iter("memcached", [

    // network address of the memcached service.

    //

    // For example: "tcp://localhost:11211"

    // ("endpoint".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // the working directory of the service. Can be "/path/to/dir"

    //

    // default is "/"

    // ("root".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // Memcached username, optional.

    // ("username".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // Memcached password, optional.

    // ("password".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // The default ttl for put operations.

    // ("default_ttl".to_string(), "10s".to_string()),


    // The maximum number of connections allowed.

    //

    // default is 10

    // ("connection_pool_max_size".to_string(), "1000".to_string()),

])?;


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General6

| Key                         | Type     | Required | Description                                                                    |
| --------------------------- | -------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| endpoint                    | string   | no       | network address of the memcached service. For example: "tcp://localhost:11211" |
| root                        | string   | no       | the working directory of the service. Can be "/path/to/dir" default is "/"     |
| username                    | string   | no       | Memcached username, optional.                                                  |
| password                    | string   | no       | Memcached password, optional.                                                  |
| default\_ttl                | duration | no       | The default ttl for put operations.                                            |
| connection\_pool\_max\_size | integer  | no       | The maximum number of connections allowed. default is 10                       |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

Copyright © 2022-2026, The Apache Software Foundation  
Apache OpenDAL, OpenDAL, Apache, the Apache feather and the Apache OpenDAL project logo are either registered trademarks or trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.

# mini-moka

4 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/mini-moka"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(mini-moka.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/mini-moka"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(mini-moka.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// Sets the max capacity of the cache.

	//

	// Refer to [`mini-moka::sync::CacheBuilder::max_capacity`](https://docs.rs/mini-moka/latest/mini_moka/sync/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.max_capacity)

	// "max_capacity": "1000",


	// Sets the time to live of the cache.

	//

	// Refer to [`mini-moka::sync::CacheBuilder::time_to_live`](https://docs.rs/mini-moka/latest/mini_moka/sync/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time_to_live)

	// "time_to_live": "...",


	// Sets the time to idle of the cache.

	//

	// Refer to [`mini-moka::sync::CacheBuilder::time_to_idle`](https://docs.rs/mini-moka/latest/mini_moka/sync/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time_to_idle)

	// "time_to_idle": "...",


	// root path of this backend

	// "root": "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General4

| Key            | Type    | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| -------------- | ------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| max\_capacity  | integer | no       | Sets the max capacity of the cache. Refer to \[mini-moka::sync::CacheBuilder::max\_capacity\](https://docs.rs/mini-moka/latest/mini\_moka/sync/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.max\_capacity)   |
| time\_to\_live | string  | no       | Sets the time to live of the cache. Refer to \[mini-moka::sync::CacheBuilder::time\_to\_live\](https://docs.rs/mini-moka/latest/mini\_moka/sync/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time\_to\_live) |
| time\_to\_idle | string  | no       | Sets the time to idle of the cache. Refer to \[mini-moka::sync::CacheBuilder::time\_to\_idle\](https://docs.rs/mini-moka/latest/mini\_moka/sync/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time\_to\_idle) |
| root           | string  | no       | root path of this backend                                                                                                                                                                       |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

```
// config.put("max_capacity", "1000");

//

// Refer to [`mini-moka::sync::CacheBuilder::time_to_live`](https://docs.rs/mini-moka/latest/mini_moka/sync/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time_to_live)

// config.put("time_to_live", "...");

// Refer to [`mini-moka::sync::CacheBuilder::time_to_idle`](https://docs.rs/mini-moka/latest/mini_moka/sync/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time_to_idle)

// config.put("time_to_idle", "...");


// root path of this backend

// config.put("root", "...");
```

| -------------- | ------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| max\_capacity  | integer | no       | Sets the max capacity of the cache. Refer to \[mini-moka::sync::CacheBuilder::max\_capacity\](https://docs.rs/mini-moka/latest/mini\_moka/sync/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.max\_capacity)   |
| time\_to\_live | string  | no       | Sets the time to live of the cache. Refer to \[mini-moka::sync::CacheBuilder::time\_to\_live\](https://docs.rs/mini-moka/latest/mini\_moka/sync/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time\_to\_live) |
| time\_to\_idle | string  | no       | Sets the time to idle of the cache. Refer to \[mini-moka::sync::CacheBuilder::time\_to\_idle\](https://docs.rs/mini-moka/latest/mini\_moka/sync/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time\_to\_idle) |
| root           | string  | no       | root path of this backend                                                                                                                                                                       |

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import opendal


operator = opendal.Operator(

    "mini-moka",

    # Sets the max capacity of the cache.

    #

    # Refer to [`mini-moka::sync::CacheBuilder::max_capacity`](https://docs.rs/mini-moka/latest/mini_moka/sync/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.max_capacity)

    # max_capacity="1000",


    # Sets the time to live of the cache.

    #

    # Refer to [`mini-moka::sync::CacheBuilder::time_to_live`](https://docs.rs/mini-moka/latest/mini_moka/sync/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time_to_live)

    # time_to_live="...",


    # Sets the time to idle of the cache.

    #

    # Refer to [`mini-moka::sync::CacheBuilder::time_to_idle`](https://docs.rs/mini-moka/latest/mini_moka/sync/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time_to_idle)

    # time_to_idle="...",


    # root path of this backend

    # root="...",

)


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

# mini-moka

4 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

use opendal::Operator;


let operator = Operator::via_iter("mini-moka", [

])?;


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

use opendal::Operator;


let operator = Operator::via_iter("mini-moka", [

    // Sets the max capacity of the cache.

    //

    // Refer to [`mini-moka::sync::CacheBuilder::max_capacity`](https://docs.rs/mini-moka/latest/mini_moka/sync/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.max_capacity)

    // ("max_capacity".to_string(), "1000".to_string()),


    // Sets the time to live of the cache.

    //

    // Refer to [`mini-moka::sync::CacheBuilder::time_to_live`](https://docs.rs/mini-moka/latest/mini_moka/sync/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time_to_live)

    // ("time_to_live".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // Sets the time to idle of the cache.

    //

    // Refer to [`mini-moka::sync::CacheBuilder::time_to_idle`](https://docs.rs/mini-moka/latest/mini_moka/sync/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time_to_idle)

    // ("time_to_idle".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // root path of this backend

    // ("root".to_string(), "...".to_string()),

])?;


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General4

| Key            | Type    | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| -------------- | ------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| max\_capacity  | integer | no       | Sets the max capacity of the cache. Refer to \[mini-moka::sync::CacheBuilder::max\_capacity\](https://docs.rs/mini-moka/latest/mini\_moka/sync/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.max\_capacity)   |
| time\_to\_live | string  | no       | Sets the time to live of the cache. Refer to \[mini-moka::sync::CacheBuilder::time\_to\_live\](https://docs.rs/mini-moka/latest/mini\_moka/sync/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time\_to\_live) |
| time\_to\_idle | string  | no       | Sets the time to idle of the cache. Refer to \[mini-moka::sync::CacheBuilder::time\_to\_idle\](https://docs.rs/mini-moka/latest/mini\_moka/sync/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time\_to\_idle) |
| root           | string  | no       | root path of this backend                                                                                                                                                                       |

# moka

5 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/moka"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(moka.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/moka"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(moka.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// Name for this cache instance.

	// "name": "...",


	// Sets the max capacity of the cache.

	//

	// Refer to [`moka::future::CacheBuilder::max_capacity`](https://docs.rs/moka/latest/moka/future/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.max_capacity)

	// "max_capacity": "1000",


	// Sets the time to live of the cache.

	//

	// Refer to [`moka::future::CacheBuilder::time_to_live`](https://docs.rs/moka/latest/moka/future/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time_to_live)

	// "time_to_live": "...",


	// Sets the time to idle of the cache.

	//

	// Refer to [`moka::future::CacheBuilder::time_to_idle`](https://docs.rs/moka/latest/moka/future/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time_to_idle)

	// "time_to_idle": "...",


	// root path of this backend

	// "root": "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General5

| Key            | Type    | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                         |
| -------------- | ------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| name           | string  | no       | Name for this cache instance.                                                                                                                                                       |
| max\_capacity  | integer | no       | Sets the max capacity of the cache. Refer to \[moka::future::CacheBuilder::max\_capacity\](https://docs.rs/moka/latest/moka/future/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.max\_capacity)   |
| time\_to\_live | string  | no       | Sets the time to live of the cache. Refer to \[moka::future::CacheBuilder::time\_to\_live\](https://docs.rs/moka/latest/moka/future/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time\_to\_live) |
| time\_to\_idle | string  | no       | Sets the time to idle of the cache. Refer to \[moka::future::CacheBuilder::time\_to\_idle\](https://docs.rs/moka/latest/moka/future/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time\_to\_idle) |
| root           | string  | no       | root path of this backend                                                                                                                                                           |

# moka

5 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

import java.util.HashMap;

```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import java.util.HashMap;

import java.util.Map;

import org.apache.opendal.Operator;


Map<String, String> config = new HashMap<>();

// Name for this cache instance.

// config.put("name", "...");


// Sets the max capacity of the cache.

//

// Refer to [`moka::future::CacheBuilder::max_capacity`](https://docs.rs/moka/latest/moka/future/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.max_capacity)

// config.put("max_capacity", "1000");


// Sets the time to live of the cache.

//

// Refer to [`moka::future::CacheBuilder::time_to_live`](https://docs.rs/moka/latest/moka/future/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time_to_live)

// config.put("time_to_live", "...");


// Sets the time to idle of the cache.

//

// Refer to [`moka::future::CacheBuilder::time_to_idle`](https://docs.rs/moka/latest/moka/future/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time_to_idle)

// config.put("time_to_idle", "...");


// root path of this backend

// config.put("root", "...");

Operator operator = Operator.of("moka", config);


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

```
  // Refer to [`moka::future::CacheBuilder::time_to_live`](https://docs.rs/moka/latest/moka/future/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time_to_live)

  // Refer to [`moka::future::CacheBuilder::time_to_idle`](https://docs.rs/moka/latest/moka/future/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time_to_idle)

  // time_to_idle: "...",


  // root path of this backend

  // root: "...",

});
```

# moka

5 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

use opendal::Operator;


let operator = Operator::via_iter("moka", [

])?;


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

use opendal::Operator;


let operator = Operator::via_iter("moka", [

    // Name for this cache instance.

    // ("name".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // Sets the max capacity of the cache.

    //

    // Refer to [`moka::future::CacheBuilder::max_capacity`](https://docs.rs/moka/latest/moka/future/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.max_capacity)

    // ("max_capacity".to_string(), "1000".to_string()),


    // Sets the time to live of the cache.

    //

    // Refer to [`moka::future::CacheBuilder::time_to_live`](https://docs.rs/moka/latest/moka/future/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time_to_live)

    // ("time_to_live".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // Sets the time to idle of the cache.

    //

    // Refer to [`moka::future::CacheBuilder::time_to_idle`](https://docs.rs/moka/latest/moka/future/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time_to_idle)

    // ("time_to_idle".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // root path of this backend

    // ("root".to_string(), "...".to_string()),

])?;


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General5

| Key            | Type    | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                         |
| -------------- | ------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| name           | string  | no       | Name for this cache instance.                                                                                                                                                       |
| max\_capacity  | integer | no       | Sets the max capacity of the cache. Refer to \[moka::future::CacheBuilder::max\_capacity\](https://docs.rs/moka/latest/moka/future/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.max\_capacity)   |
| time\_to\_live | string  | no       | Sets the time to live of the cache. Refer to \[moka::future::CacheBuilder::time\_to\_live\](https://docs.rs/moka/latest/moka/future/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time\_to\_live) |
| time\_to\_idle | string  | no       | Sets the time to idle of the cache. Refer to \[moka::future::CacheBuilder::time\_to\_idle\](https://docs.rs/moka/latest/moka/future/struct.CacheBuilder.html#method.time\_to\_idle) |
| root           | string  | no       | root path of this backend                                                                                                                                                           |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

Apache OpenDAL, OpenDAL, Apache, the Apache feather and the Apache OpenDAL project logo are either registered trademarks or trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.

```
})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/mongodb"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(mongodb.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// connection string of this backend

	// "connection_string": "...",


	// database of this backend

	// "database": "...",


	// collection of this backend

	// "collection": "...",


	// root of this backend

	// "root": "...",


	// key field of this backend

	// "key_field": "...",


	// value field of this backend

	// "value_field": "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

| ------------------ | ------ | -------- | --------------------------------- |

| database           | string | no       | database of this backend          |
| collection         | string | no       | collection of this backend        |
| root               | string | no       | root of this backend              |
| key\_field         | string | no       | key field of this backend         |
| value\_field       | string | no       | value field of this backend       |

# monoiofs

1 configuration option · available in Rust, Python, Node.js, Go

RustPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/monoiofs"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(monoiofs.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/monoiofs"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(monoiofs.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// The Root of this backend.

	//

	// All operations will happen under this root.

	//

	// Builder::build will return error if not set.

	// "root": "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General1

| Key  | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                                        |
| ---- | ------ | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| root | string | no       | The Root of this backend. All operations will happen under this root. Builder::build will return error if not set. |

# mysql

5 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsGo

Quick start

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/mysql"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(mysql.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/mysql"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(mysql.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// This connection string is used to connect to the mysql service. There are url based formats.

	//

	// The format of connect string resembles the url format of the mysql client.

	// The format is: `[scheme://][user[:[password]]@]host[:port][/schema][?attribute1=value1&attribute2=value2...`

	//

	// - `mysql://user@localhost`

	// - `mysql://user:password@localhost`

	// - `mysql://user:password@localhost:3306`

	// - `mysql://user:password@localhost:3306/db`

	//

	// For more information, please refer to <https://docs.rs/sqlx/latest/sqlx/mysql/struct.MySqlConnectOptions.html>.

	// "connection_string": "...",


	// The table name for mysql.

	// "table": "...",


	// The key field name for mysql.

	// "key_field": "...",


	// The value field name for mysql.

	// "value_field": "...",


	// The root for mysql.

	// "root": "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General5

| Key                | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| ------------------ | ------ | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| connection\_string | string | no       | This connection string is used to connect to the mysql service. There are url based formats. The format of connect string resembles the url format of the mysql client. The format is: \[scheme://\]\[user\[:\[password\]\]@\]host\[:port\]\[/schema\]\[?attribute1=value1&attribute2=value2...\- mysql://user@localhost\- mysql://user:password@localhost\- mysql://user:password@localhost:3306\- mysql://user:password@localhost:3306/dbFor more information, please refer to <https://docs.rs/sqlx/latest/sqlx/mysql/struct.MySqlConnectOptions.html>. |
| table              | string | no       | The table name for mysql.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| key\_field         | string | no       | The key field name for mysql.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| value\_field       | string | no       | The value field name for mysql.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| root               | string | no       | The root for mysql.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import java.util.HashMap;

import java.util.Map;

import org.apache.opendal.Operator;


Map<String, String> config = new HashMap<>();

// This connection string is used to connect to the mysql service. There are url based formats.

//

// The format of connect string resembles the url format of the mysql client.

// The format is: `[scheme://][user[:[password]]@]host[:port][/schema][?attribute1=value1&attribute2=value2...`

//

// - `mysql://user@localhost`

// - `mysql://user:password@localhost`

// - `mysql://user:password@localhost:3306`

// - `mysql://user:password@localhost:3306/db`

//

// For more information, please refer to <https://docs.rs/sqlx/latest/sqlx/mysql/struct.MySqlConnectOptions.html>.

// config.put("connection_string", "...");


// The table name for mysql.

// config.put("table", "...");


// The key field name for mysql.

// config.put("key_field", "...");


// The value field name for mysql.

// config.put("value_field", "...");


// The root for mysql.

// config.put("root", "...");

Operator operator = Operator.of("mysql", config);


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

The format is: \[scheme://\]\[user\[:\[password\]\]@\]host\[:port\]\[/schema\]\[?attribute1=value1&attribute2=value2...\- mysql://user@localhost\- mysql://user:password@localhost\- mysql://user:password@localhost:3306\- mysql://user:password@localhost:3306/dbFor more information, please refer to <https://docs.rs/sqlx/latest/sqlx/mysql/struct.MySqlConnectOptions.html>. |
| table              | string | no       | The table name for mysql.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| key\_field         | string | no       | The key field name for mysql.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| value\_field       | string | no       | The value field name for mysql.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |

```
  // - `mysql://user:password@localhost`

  // - `mysql://user:password@localhost:3306/db`

  //

  // For more information, please refer to <https://docs.rs/sqlx/latest/sqlx/mysql/struct.MySqlConnectOptions.html>.

  // connection_string: "...",


  // The table name for mysql.

  // table: "...",


  // The key field name for mysql.

  // key_field: "...",


  // The value field name for mysql.

  // value_field: "...",


  // The root for mysql.

  // root: "...",

});


```

# obs

6 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsRubyGo

Quick start

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/obs"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(obs.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import (

	"github.com/apache/opendal-go-services/obs"

	opendal "github.com/apache/opendal/bindings/go"

)


operator, err := opendal.NewOperator(obs.Scheme, opendal.OperatorOptions{

	// Root for obs.

	// "root": "...",


	// Endpoint for obs.

	// "endpoint": "...",


	// Access key id for obs.

	// "access_key_id": "...",


	// Secret access key for obs.

	// "secret_access_key": "...",


	// Bucket for obs.

	// "bucket": "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General6

| Key                          | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ---------------------------- | ------ | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| root                         | string | no       | Root for obs.                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| endpoint                     | string | no       | Endpoint for obs.                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| access\_key\_id              | string | no       | Access key id for obs.                                                                                                                                                                             |
| secret\_access\_key          | string | no       | Secret access key for obs.                                                                                                                                                                         |
| bucket                       | string | no       | Bucket for obs.                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| enable\_versioningdeprecated | bool   | no       | Deprecated: OBS versioning capability is not controlled by service config.Deprecated since 0.57.0: OBS versioning capability is not controlled by this option and this option is no longer needed. |

# obs

6 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsRubyGo

Quick start

```

import java.util.HashMap;

import java.util.Map;

import org.apache.opendal.Operator;


Map<String, String> config = new HashMap<>();

Operator operator = Operator.of("obs", config);


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import java.util.HashMap;

import java.util.Map;

import org.apache.opendal.Operator;


Map<String, String> config = new HashMap<>();

// Root for obs.

// config.put("root", "...");


// Endpoint for obs.

// config.put("endpoint", "...");


// Access key id for obs.

// config.put("access_key_id", "...");


// Secret access key for obs.

// config.put("secret_access_key", "...");


// Bucket for obs.

// config.put("bucket", "...");

Operator operator = Operator.of("obs", config);


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General6

| Key                          | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ---------------------------- | ------ | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| root                         | string | no       | Root for obs.                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| endpoint                     | string | no       | Endpoint for obs.                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| access\_key\_id              | string | no       | Access key id for obs.                                                                                                                                                                             |
| secret\_access\_key          | string | no       | Secret access key for obs.                                                                                                                                                                         |
| bucket                       | string | no       | Bucket for obs.                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| enable\_versioningdeprecated | bool   | no       | Deprecated: OBS versioning capability is not controlled by service config.Deprecated since 0.57.0: OBS versioning capability is not controlled by this option and this option is no longer needed. |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

Copyright © 2022-2026, The Apache Software Foundation  
Apache OpenDAL, OpenDAL, Apache, the Apache feather and the Apache OpenDAL project logo are either registered trademarks or trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.

url: https://opendal.apache.org/services/obs/nodejs/

# obs

6 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsRubyGo

Quick start

```

import { Operator } from "opendal";


const operator = new Operator("obs", {

});


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import { Operator } from "opendal";


const operator = new Operator("obs", {

  // Root for obs.

  // root: "...",


  // Endpoint for obs.

  // endpoint: "...",


  // Access key id for obs.

  // access_key_id: "...",


  // Secret access key for obs.

  // secret_access_key: "...",


  // Bucket for obs.

  // bucket: "...",

});


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General6

| Key                          | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ---------------------------- | ------ | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| root                         | string | no       | Root for obs.                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| endpoint                     | string | no       | Endpoint for obs.                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| access\_key\_id              | string | no       | Access key id for obs.                                                                                                                                                                             |
| secret\_access\_key          | string | no       | Secret access key for obs.                                                                                                                                                                         |
| bucket                       | string | no       | Bucket for obs.                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| enable\_versioningdeprecated | bool   | no       | Deprecated: OBS versioning capability is not controlled by service config.Deprecated since 0.57.0: OBS versioning capability is not controlled by this option and this option is no longer needed. |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

Copyright © 2022-2026, The Apache Software Foundation  
Apache OpenDAL, OpenDAL, Apache, the Apache feather and the Apache OpenDAL project logo are either registered trademarks or trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.

# obs

6 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsRubyGo

Quick start

```

import opendal


operator = opendal.Operator(

    "obs",

)


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

import opendal


operator = opendal.Operator(

    "obs",

    # Root for obs.

    # root="...",


    # Endpoint for obs.

    # endpoint="...",


    # Access key id for obs.

    # access_key_id="...",


    # Secret access key for obs.

    # secret_access_key="...",


    # Bucket for obs.

    # bucket="...",

)


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General6

| Key                          | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ---------------------------- | ------ | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| root                         | string | no       | Root for obs.                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| endpoint                     | string | no       | Endpoint for obs.                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| access\_key\_id              | string | no       | Access key id for obs.                                                                                                                                                                             |
| secret\_access\_key          | string | no       | Secret access key for obs.                                                                                                                                                                         |
| bucket                       | string | no       | Bucket for obs.                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| enable\_versioningdeprecated | bool   | no       | Deprecated: OBS versioning capability is not controlled by service config.Deprecated since 0.57.0: OBS versioning capability is not controlled by this option and this option is no longer needed. |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

Copyright © 2022-2026, The Apache Software Foundation  
Apache OpenDAL, OpenDAL, Apache, the Apache feather and the Apache OpenDAL project logo are either registered trademarks or trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.

# obs

6 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsRubyGo

Quick start

```

require "opendal"


operator = OpenDal::Operator.new("obs", {

})


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

require "opendal"


operator = OpenDal::Operator.new("obs", {

  # Root for obs.

  # "root" => "...",


  # Endpoint for obs.

  # "endpoint" => "...",


  # Access key id for obs.

  # "access_key_id" => "...",


  # Secret access key for obs.

  # "secret_access_key" => "...",


  # Bucket for obs.

  # "bucket" => "...",

})


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General6

| Key                          | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ---------------------------- | ------ | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| root                         | string | no       | Root for obs.                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| endpoint                     | string | no       | Endpoint for obs.                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| access\_key\_id              | string | no       | Access key id for obs.                                                                                                                                                                             |
| secret\_access\_key          | string | no       | Secret access key for obs.                                                                                                                                                                         |
| bucket                       | string | no       | Bucket for obs.                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| enable\_versioningdeprecated | bool   | no       | Deprecated: OBS versioning capability is not controlled by service config.Deprecated since 0.57.0: OBS versioning capability is not controlled by this option and this option is no longer needed. |

# obs

6 configuration options · available in Rust, Java, Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go

RustJavaPythonNode.jsRubyGo

Quick start

```

use opendal::Operator;


let operator = Operator::via_iter("obs", [

])?;


```

All configuration options (copy & trim)

Full reference

```

use opendal::Operator;


let operator = Operator::via_iter("obs", [

    // Root for obs.

    // ("root".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // Endpoint for obs.

    // ("endpoint".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // Access key id for obs.

    // ("access_key_id".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // Secret access key for obs.

    // ("secret_access_key".to_string(), "...".to_string()),


    // Bucket for obs.

    // ("bucket".to_string(), "...".to_string()),

])?;


```

Every option is passed as a string key; OpenDAL parses it into the right type. Some services may require building the binding with the matching `services-*` feature enabled.

## Configuration reference

General6

| Key                          | Type   | Required | Description                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ---------------------------- | ------ | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| root                         | string | no       | Root for obs.                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| endpoint                     | string | no       | Endpoint for obs.                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| access\_key\_id              | string | no       | Access key id for obs.                                                                                                                                                                             |
| secret\_access\_key          | string | no       | Secret access key for obs.                                                                                                                                                                         |
| bucket                       | string | no       | Bucket for obs.                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| enable\_versioningdeprecated | bool   | no       | Deprecated: OBS versioning capability is not controlled by service config.Deprecated since 0.57.0: OBS versioning capability is not controlled by this option and this option is no longer needed. |

[![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)![Apache Software Foundation](/./img/asf_logo_wide.svg)](https://www.apache.org/)

Apache OpenDAL, OpenDAL, Apache, the Apache feather and the Apache OpenDAL project logo are either registered trademarks or trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.
